what: Use case 2: Study of mosaic PBP2 mutations associated with decreased susceptibility and resistance to ceftriaxone
title: Analysing Neisseria gonorrhoeae genotype-phenotype data
text: use case, explore combinations PBP2 mutations associated MIC ranges mosaic penA variants, using broader dataset enriches ceftriaxone-resistant decreased-susceptibility isolates. dataset combines AMRFinderPlus results following sources (total n = 2,101 genomes): ENA PRJEB58139, n=1,932 genomes. ENA PRJEB57389, n=8 genomes. ENA PRJEB76977, n=19 genomes. ENA PRJEB45627, PRJNA577446, PRJNA776899, PRJNA778600, PRJNA560592, PRJNA874857, PRJNA909328, PRJNA957547, PRJNA1161034, PRJNA1189294, n=113 genomes. ENA PRJNA1067895, n=29 genomes. Import format data: Build binary matrix generate upset plots:  Calculate combination PPVs:  Run logistic regression evaluate individual marker contributions:  Results confirm individual penA mutations independently increase cephalosporin MICs; rather, specific combinations mutations required. penA I312M wide coefficient interval, likely reflecting contribution penA mosaics associated resistance.
code: # Genotype file ngono_cro_geno <- import_amrfp(ngono_cro_geno_raw, "Name") #> Input file lacks the expected column: 'Type' (v4.0+) or 'Element type' (pre-v4), assuming all rows report AMR markers.  # Phenotype file ngono_cro_pheno <- ngono_cro_pheno_raw %>%   pivot_longer(     cols = c(Ceftriaxone),     names_to = "antibiotic",     values_to = "mic"   )  ngono_cro_ast <- format_ast(   input = ngono_cro_pheno,   sample_col = "id",   species = "Neisseria gonorrhoeae",   ab_col = "antibiotic",   mic_col = "mic",   interpret_eucast = TRUE ) #> Adding new micro-organism column 'spp_pheno' (class 'mo') with constant value Neisseria gonorrhoeae  #> Parsing column spp_pheno as micro-organism (class 'mo') #> Parsing column antibiotic as antibiotic (class 'ab') #> Renaming column antibiotic to standard name 'drug_agent' #> Parsing column mic as class 'mic' #> Could not find disk_col disk in input table #> Could not find pheno_col ecoff in input table #> Could not find pheno_col pheno_eucast in input table #> Could not find pheno_col pheno_clsi in input table #> Could not find pheno_col pheno_provided in input table #> Could not find method_col method in input table #> Could not find platform_col platform in input table #> Could not find guideline_col guideline in input table #> Could not find source_col source in input table #> Interpreting all data as species: Neisseria gonorrhoeae  # Include empty rows for samples with phenotype but no genotype data negative_cro <- ngono_cro_pheno_raw %>%   anti_join(ngono_cro_geno, by = c("id" = "Name")) %>%   pull(id)  ngono_cro_geno <- ngono_cro_geno %>% bind_rows(tibble(Name = negative_cro)) # Get binary matrix cro_bin_2 <- get_binary_matrix(   geno_table = ngono_cro_geno,   pheno_table = ngono_cro_ast,   antibiotic = "Ceftriaxone",   drug_class_list = "Cephalosporins (3rd gen.)",   sir_col = "pheno_eucast",   keep_assay_values = TRUE,   keep_assay_values_from = "mic" ) #>  Defining NWT in binary matrix as I/R vs S, as no ECOFF column defined  # Calculate upset plots of MIC vs genotype marker combinations cro_upset_2 <- amr_upset(   binary_matrix = cro_bin_2,   min_set_size = 1,   order = "value",   assay = "mic",   antibiotic = "Ceftriaxone",   species = "Neisseria gonorrhoeae" ) #>   MIC breakpoints determined using AMR package: S <= 0.125 and R > 0.125 #>   MIC breakpoints determined using AMR package: S <= 0.125 and R > 0.125 cro_ppv_2 <- ppv(   binary_matrix = cro_bin_2,   min_set_size = 1,   order = "ppv",   upset_grid = TRUE,   plot_assay = TRUE,   assay = "mic" ) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale. cro_logist <- amr_logistic(   binary_matrix = cro_bin_2,   antibiotic = "Ceftriaxone",   sir_col = "pheno_eucast",   ecoff_col = "ecoff",   fit_glm = TRUE,   maf = 10,   single_plot = TRUE ) #> ...Fitting logistic regression model to R using glm #>    Filtered data contains 2191 samples (211 => 1, 1980 => 0) and 13 variables. #> Waiting for profiling to be done... #> ...Fitting logistic regression model to NWT using glm #>    Filtered data contains 2191 samples (211 => 1, 1980 => 0) and 13 variables. #> Waiting for profiling to be done... #> Generating plots #> Plotting 2 models

what: Use case 3: Investigation of tetracycline resistance and implications for STI prevention strategies
title: Analysing Neisseria gonorrhoeae genotype-phenotype data
text: Doxy-PEP (doxycycline post-exposure prophylaxis) involves taking single dose doxycycline within 24–72 hours sexual risk event prevent STIs. evidence supports short-term efficacy, modelling study Reichert et al. (2026) suggests broad doxy-PEP implementation may select resistant lineages, potentially limiting long-term effectiveness gonorrhoea. , analyse 409 N. gonorrhoeae genomes isolates collected eastern Spain 2021–2024 (Sánchez-Serrano et al., 2026, ENA PRJEB83795), available tetracycline MIC data, explore genetic determinants tetracycline resistance local gonococcal population. Import format phenotype data: Import genotype data add empty rows samples phenotype information genetic AMR determinants identified AMRfinderplus. example, 409 samples MIC phenotype file AMR determinants found 402. Build binary matrix generate upset plots:  upset plot shows MIC increases primarily explained chromosomal rpsJ V57M mutation tet(M) gene, carried conjugative plasmid. Calculate prevalence two key resistance determinants studied population: 409 isolates tetracycline phenotypic data: 373 (91.2%) carry rpsJ V57M chromosomal mutation. 34 (8.3%) carry tet(M) gene conjugative plasmid. Explore marker combinations upset summary: Calculate PPVs marker combinations:  Run logistic regression confirm independent contributions rpsJ V57M tet(M):  ECOFF defined tetracycline N. gonorrhoeae, use clinical breakpoints (truth = "R") concordance analysis: current marker set achieves 98.72% sensitivity 82.84% PPV, 28.09 % specificity. reflected high major error rate (= 71.91%), meaning many susceptible isolates predicted resistant — consequence high prevalence rpsJ V57M isolates elevated MICs remain clinical breakpoint. Visualise predictions MIC distribution:  high prevalence rpsJ V57M (>90% population) combined significant prevalence plasmid-borne tet(M) gene — can readily acquired circulating lineages conjugation — means doxy-PEP likely select pre-existing resistant lineages. Together, findings support conclusion doxy-PEP unlikely provide durable long-term solution reducing burden gonorrhoea infections.
code: ngono_tet_pheno <- ngono_tet_pheno_raw %>%   pivot_longer(     cols = c(Tetracycline),     names_to = "antibiotic",     values_to = "mic"   )  ngono_tet_ast <- format_ast(   input = ngono_tet_pheno,   sample_col = "id",   species = "Neisseria gonorrhoeae",   ab_col = "antibiotic",   mic_col = "mic",   interpret_eucast = TRUE,   interpret_ecoff = TRUE ) #> Adding new micro-organism column 'spp_pheno' (class 'mo') with constant value Neisseria gonorrhoeae  #> Parsing column spp_pheno as micro-organism (class 'mo') #> Parsing column antibiotic as antibiotic (class anti_join(ngono_tet_geno, by = c("id" = "Name")) %>%   pull(id)  ngono_tet_geno <- ngono_tet_geno %>% bind_rows(tibble(Name = negative_samples)) tet_bin <- get_binary_matrix(   geno_table = ngono_tet_geno,   pheno_table = ngono_tet_ast,   antibiotic = "Tetracycline",   drug_class_list = "Tetracyclines",   ecoff_col = "ecoff",   sir_col = "pheno_eucast",   keep_assay_values = TRUE,   keep_assay_values_from = "mic" ) #>  Defining NWT in binary matrix using ecoff column provided: ecoff  tet_upset <- amr_upset(   binary_matrix = tet_bin,   assay = "mic",   min_set_size = 2,   order = "value",   bp_R = 0.5,   plot_set_size = TRUE,   print_set_size = TRUE,   print_category_counts = TRUE ) pop_size <- 409  ngono_tet_geno %>%   count(marker) %>%   filter(marker %in% c("rpsJ_V57M", "tet(M)")) %>%   mutate(percent = round(100 * n / pop_size, 1)) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   marker        n percent #>   <chr>     <int>   <dbl> #> 1 rpsJ_V57M   373    91.2 #> 2 tet(M)       34     8.3 title: Analysing a small user-generated Salmonella geno-pheno dataset
text: vignette shows AMRgen can used explore small user-generated dataset genotypic phenotypic data non-typhoidal, non-invasive Salmonella enterica isolates. Phenotypic data consists MIC E-test results ciprofloxacin, levofloxacin, moxifloxacin. Genotypic data includes AMRFinderPlus results quinolone resistance markers. Start loading package:
code: library(AMRgen) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(ggplot2) library(tidyr)

title: Analysing a small user-generated Salmonella geno-pheno dataset
text: example, data table exported user’s laboratory information management system single .csv file includes genotypic phenotypic results 115 S. enterica isolates, one line per isolate. columns two categorical variables interest: isolate source (human, animal, ) isolate serovar. Genotype data stored single column, multiple quinolone resistance markers identified, listed string semicolon delimiters. Phenotype data stored separate columns antimicrobial agent tested (ciprofloxacin, levofloxacin, moxifloxacin). AMRgen package requires separate phenotypic genotypic tables specific column headers, next step separate wrangle data required formats.
code: # Raw data table salm_raw <- read_csv(here::here("data", "Salmonella_pheno_geno_data.csv")) head(salm_raw) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   Sample Source Serovar     CpL_Genotype Ciprofloxacin Levofloxacin Moxifloxacin #>   <chr>  <chr>  <chr>       <chr>        <chr>         <chr>        <chr>        #> 1 SAL001 Animal other       gyrA_S83F;p… 0.19          0.5          0.5          #> 2 SAL002 Human  S. Enterit… gyrA_S83Y    0.38          1.5          1.5          #> 3 SAL003 Human  other       gyrA_S83F;p… 3             12           24           #> 4 SAL004 other  S. Infantis gyrA_D87G    0.125         0.5          0.5          #> 5 SAL005 Human  other       gyrA_D87Y    0.094         0.25         0.38         #> 6 SAL006 Human  S. Kentucky gyrA_S83F;g… 8             8            32

title: Analysing a small user-generated Salmonella geno-pheno dataset
text: use downstream AMRgen functions, genotype table must minimum following columns: Name (unique sample name isolate), marker (name resistance marker detected), drug_class (antibiotic class associated marker). also needs long form, row isolate/marker combination. dataset, genotype information found single column alongside metadata phenotypic information, can multiple markers per row (four). , carry following steps generate genotype table compatible AMRgen functions: * Select genotype column (called CpL_Genotype dataset) column specifying individual isolate names (called Sample dataset) * Rename column Sample Name, default column heading used AMRgen identifying individual isolates * Separate genotype column CpL_Genotype individual columns marker (four per isolate data), specifying delimiter ; * Convert wide-form data (one row per isolate) long-form table (one row per isolate/marker combination) using pivot_longer * Add column specifying markers associated resistance quinolones
code: # Extract geno data, separate by delimiter, pivot longer, and add drug_class column salm_geno <- salm_raw %>%   select(Sample, CpL_Genotype) %>%   rename(Name = Sample) %>%   separate_wider_delim(CpL_Genotype,     delim = ";", names = c("Marker_1", "Marker_2", "Marker_3", "Marker_4"),     too_few = "align_start", cols_remove = TRUE   ) %>%   pivot_longer(     cols = c(Marker_1, Marker_2, Marker_3, Marker_4),     names_to = "marker_no",     values_to = "marker",     values_drop_na = TRUE   ) %>%   select(-marker_no) %>%   mutate(     drug_class = "Quinolones",     drug_agent = NA_character_   ) ## the variable drug_agent appears essential for get_binary_matrix function to work...   # Check the format of the processed genotype table head(salm_geno) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   Name   marker    drug_class drug_agent #>   <chr>  <chr>     <chr>      <chr>      #> 1 SAL001 gyrA_S83F Quinolones NA         #> 2 SAL001 parC_T57S Quinolones NA         #> 3 SAL002 gyrA_S83Y Quinolones NA         #> 4 SAL003 gyrA_S83F Quinolones NA         #> 5 SAL003 parC_T57S Quinolones NA         #> 6 SAL003 qnrB19    Quinolones NA

title: Analysing a small user-generated Salmonella geno-pheno dataset
text: use downstream AMRgen functions, phenotype table must long form single column antimicrobial agents tested, rather separate columns agent. also needs minimum following columns: id (unique sample name isolate), spp_pheno (species name formatted per AMR package mo class), drug_agent (antimicrobial agent name formatted per AMR package ab class), S//R phenotype column (e.g. one pheno_eucast, pheno_clsi, pheno_provided, ecoff). raw assay data included, needs column called mic /disk. convert dataset format, begin extracting columns unique isolate names (Sample) MIC results three antimicrobial agents tested (Ciprofloxacin, Levofloxacin, Moxifloxacin). also keep columns additional metadata (Source, Serovar), genotype table. antimicrobial columns need forced character vectors avoid issues caused occasional presence non-numeric prefixes (> <). pivot table long form. phenotype data yet standard AMR/AMRgen format use helpful format_ast function add species name, format antimicrobial MIC columns correctly, generate S//R phenotype column pheno_eucast interpreting Salmonella MIC data EUCAST breakpoints. apply breakpoints across human/animal/isolates, interest resistance phenotypes potentially problematic human infections, including zoonotic ones. Now formatted data genotype table (salm_geno) phenotype table (salm_ast) compatible AMRgen, can use downstream functions plotting distributions, combining geno/pheno data, modeling binary drug phenotype, assessing positive predictive values genetic markers, comparing combinations markers influence resistance phenotype. vignettes outline workflows detail, show basic workflow, focusing additional metadata can included.
code: # Pheno table: select columns with sample ID, metadata, and antimicrobials tested, then pivot to long form salm_pheno <- salm_raw %>%   select(Sample, Source, Serovar, Ciprofloxacin, Levofloxacin, Moxifloxacin) %>%   mutate(across(c(Ciprofloxacin, Levofloxacin, Moxifloxacin), as.character)) %>%   pivot_longer(     cols = c(Ciprofloxacin, Levofloxacin, Moxifloxacin),     names_to = "antibiotic",     values_to = "MIC.values",     values_drop_na = TRUE   ) salm_ast <- format_ast(   input = salm_pheno,   sample_col = "Sample",   species = "Salmonella enterica",   ab_col = "antibiotic",   mic_col = "MIC.values",   interpret_eucast = TRUE ) #> Adding new micro-organism column 'spp_pheno' (class 'mo') with constant value Salmonella enterica  #> Parsing column spp_pheno as micro-organism (class 'mo') #> Parsing column antibiotic as antibiotic (class 'ab') #> Renaming column antibiotic to standard name 'drug_agent' #> Parsing column MIC.values as class 'mic' #> Renaming column MIC.values to standard name 'mic' #> Could not find disk_col disk in input table #> Could not find pheno_col ecoff in input table #> Could not find pheno_col pheno_eucast in input table #> Could not find pheno_col pheno_clsi in input table #> Could not find pheno_col pheno_provided in input table #> Could not find method_col method in input table #> Could not find platform_col platform in input table #> Could not find guideline_col guideline in input table #> Could not find source_col source in input table #> Interpreting all data as species: Salmonella enterica  # Check the format of the processed phenotype table head(salm_ast) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   id     drug_agent   mic pheno_eucast spp_pheno    Source Serovar        #>   <chr>  <ab>       <mic> <sir>        <mo>         <chr>  <chr>          #> 1 SAL001 CIP         0.19   R          B_SLMNL_ENTR Animal other          #> 2 SAL001 LVX         0.50   S          B_SLMNL_ENTR Animal other          #> 3 SAL001 MFX         0.50   R          B_SLMNL_ENTR Animal other          #> 4 SAL002 CIP         0.38   R          B_SLMNL_ENTR Human  S. Enteritidis #> 5 SAL002 LVX         1.50   R          B_SLMNL_ENTR Human  S. Enteritidis #> 6 SAL002 MFX         1.50   R          B_SLMNL_ENTR Human  S. Enteritidis

title: Analysing a small user-generated Salmonella geno-pheno dataset
text: assay_by_var function can separate plots additional categorical variable specifying facet_var within function. example, can split Ciprofloxacin plot isolation source like :  Alternatively, assay_by_var ggplot2 function, can extended adding ggplot2 layers, including facet_wrap() single faceting variable (allow control facets displayed) facet_grid() facet two variables.  Another option displaying categorical metadata change colour variable colour_by still show breakpoints vertical lines using guideline species calls within assay_by_var. desired, can also modify aspects plot using standard ggplot2 extensions (e.g. using viridis change colour palette).
code: assay_by_var(pheno_table = salm_ast, antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin", measure = "mic", colour_by = "pheno_eucast", facet_var = "Source") assay_by_var(pheno_table = salm_ast, antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin", measure = "mic", colour_by = "pheno_eucast") +   facet_grid(Source ~ Serovar) # Specify species and guideline to show breakpoints, but colour bars by isolation source assay_by_var(pheno_table = salm_ast, antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin", measure = "mic", colour_by = "Source", species = "Salmonella enterica", guideline = "EUCAST 2025") +   scale_fill_viridis_d(end = 0.8) #>   MIC breakpoints determined using AMR package: S <= 0.064 and R > 0.064

what: Combine ciprofloxacin genotype and phenotype data
title: Analysing a small user-generated Salmonella geno-pheno dataset
text: Analysis combined genotype-phenotype data must carried separately antimicrobial agent. first step generate combined dataframe specified agent genotype phenotype tables, using get_binary_matrix() function. example, ciprofloxacin small S. enterica dataset, also done levofloxacin moxifloxacin. binary matrix can used numerous downstream analyses, described vignettes. , show can modified additional metadata variables. get_binary_matrix() output (cip_bin) retain additional metadata variables Source Serovar. use variables downstream analyses, first extract salm_pheno join cip_bin create cip_bin_meta, can used plotting functions (warning: statistical model functions may work correctly additional metadata cip_bin used instead cip_bin_meta).
code: ### get_binary_matrix throws an error unless the variable drug_class is in salm_geno, even if it's empty cip_bin <- get_binary_matrix(   salm_geno,   salm_ast,   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   drug_class_list = "Quinolones",   sir_col = "pheno_eucast",   keep_assay_values = TRUE,   keep_assay_values_from = "mic" ) #>  Defining NWT in binary matrix as I/R vs S, as no ECOFF column defined   # check format head(cip_bin) #> # A tibble: 6 × 31 #>   id     pheno   mic     R   NWT gyrA_S83F parC_T57S gyrA_S83Y qnrB19 gyrA_D87G #>   <chr>  <sir> <mic> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>  <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 SAL001   R   0.190     1     1         1         1         0      0         0 #> 2 SAL002   R   0.380     1     1         0         0         1      0         0 #> 3 SAL003   R   3.000     1     1         1         1         0      1         0 #> 4 SAL004   R   0.125     1     1         0         0         0      0         1 #> 5 SAL005   R   0.094     1     1         0         0         0      0         0 #> 6 SAL006   R   8.000     1     1         1         0         0      0         0 #> # ℹ 21 more variables: gyrA_D87Y <dbl>, gyrA_D87N <dbl>, qnrS1 <dbl>, #> #   qnrA1 <dbl>, gyrA_D87X <dbl>, qepA1 <dbl>, parC_S80I <dbl>, gyrA_u <dbl>, #> #   gyrA_D82N <dbl>, qnrB2 <dbl>, qnrD <dbl>, gyrB_E446D <dbl>, qnrDv1 <dbl>, #> #   parE_S474G <dbl>, gyrA_Y100H <dbl>, parC_S80X <dbl>, parC_F60L <dbl>, #> #   parE_D475Y <dbl>, qnrS4 <dbl>, gyrB_L446M <dbl>, parC_A81X <dbl>  # list colnames (alphabetically) to see full list of quinolone markers in data # (will include additional columns id, pheno, mic, NWT, R, Source, and Serovar) sort(colnames(cip_bin)) #>  [1] "gyrA_D82N"  "gyrA_D87G"  "gyrA_D87N"  "gyrA_D87X"  "gyrA_D87Y"  #>  [6] "gyrA_S83F"  "gyrA_S83Y"  "gyrA_u"     "gyrA_Y100H" "gyrB_E446D" #> [11] "gyrB_L446M" "id"         "mic"        "NWT"        "parC_A81X"  #> [16] "parC_F60L"  "parC_S80I"  "parC_S80X"  "parC_T57S"  "parE_D475Y" #> [21] "parE_S474G" "pheno"      "qepA1"      "qnrA1"      "qnrB19"     #> [26] "qnrB2"      "qnrD"       "qnrDv1"     "qnrS1"      "qnrS4"      #> [31] "R" salm_metadata <- salm_pheno %>%   select(Sample, Source, Serovar) %>%   rename(id = Sample)  cip_bin_meta <- left_join(cip_bin, salm_metadata) #> Joining with `by = join_by(id)`

what: Plot ciprofloxacin phenotype by number of mutations and Serovar/Source
title: Analysing a small user-generated Salmonella geno-pheno dataset
text: Joining metadata cip_bin allows us colour MIC distribution plot number mutations gyrA facet Serovar, highlighting S. Infantis isolates one gyrA mutation S. Kentucky isolates two gyrA mutations, whereas serovars variable numbers mutations.  Similarly, can plot total number markers per isolate facet Source.
code: # count the number of gyrA mutations per genome gyrA_mut <- cip_bin_meta %>%   dplyr::mutate(gyrA_mut = rowSums(across(contains("gyrA_") & where(is.numeric)), na.rm = T)) %>%   select(mic, gyrA_mut, Source, Serovar)  # plot the MIC distribution, coloured by count of gyrA mutations mic_by_gyrA_count <- assay_by_var(gyrA_mut, measure = "mic", colour_by = "gyrA_mut", colour_legend_label = "No. gyrA mutations", antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin") + facet_wrap(~Serovar) #> WARNING: Column 'drug_agent' not found in phenotype table, so can't input matrix to specified antibiotic. #> Ensure your input table is already filtered to the antibiotic.  mic_by_gyrA_count # count the number of genetic determinants per genome marker_count <- cip_bin_meta %>%   mutate(marker_count = rowSums(across(where(is.numeric) & !any_of(c("R", "NWT"))), na.rm = T)) %>%   select(mic, marker_count, Source, Serovar)  # plot the MIC distribution, coloured by count of associated genetic markers mic_by_marker_count <- assay_by_var(marker_count, measure = "mic", colour_by = "marker_count", colour_legend_label = "No. markers detected", antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin", bar_cols = viridisLite::viridis(max(marker_count$marker_count) + 1)) +   facet_wrap(~Source, ncol = 1) #> WARNING: Column 'drug_agent' not found in phenotype table, so can't input matrix to specified antibiotic. #> Ensure your input table is already filtered to the antibiotic.  mic_by_marker_count

text: can look combinations markers associated phenotypic ciprofloxacin MIC generating UpSet plot amr_upset function. function accept extra metadata columns use cip_bin instead cip_bin_meta . dataset quite small, keep combinations, including single isolate (min_set_size = 1).  can generate UpSet plots subset isolates filtering one additional metadata variables running amr_upset(). example, can focus human isolates :
code: # Compare ciprofloxacin MIC data with quinolone marker combinations, #    using the binary matrix we constructed earlier via get_binary_matrix() cipro_mic_upset <- amr_upset(   cip_bin,   min_set_size = 1,   assay = "mic",   order = "value" ) cip_bin_human <- cip_bin_meta %>%   filter(Source == "Human") %>%   select(-Source, -Serovar)  cipro_mic_upset_human <- amr_upset(   cip_bin_human,   min_set_size = 2,   assay = "mic",   order = "value" )

title: Analysing clindamycin resistance in _Staphylococcus aureus_
text: document summarises analysis clindamycin resistance S. aureus. specifically, look distribution clindamycin resistance S. aureus isolates dataset clinical samples. Start loading package:
code: library(AMRgen) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)

title: Analysing clindamycin resistance in _Staphylococcus aureus_
text: example, collated genotype-phenotype data NCBI, EBI ESGEM-AMR Staphylococcus subgroup. Phenotypic data collated single table (one row per isolate). genotype data generated using AMRFinderPlus. pre-loaded objects ast_CLI afp_CLI serve input AMRgen. First, create new marker.label column includes marker gene closest accession number (named variant now ).
code: # Create the new marker.label column cli_accession <- afp_CLI_public %>%   mutate(obs_id = row_number()) %>%   mutate(exact_match = if_else(     `% Coverage of reference sequence` == 100 &       `% Identity to reference sequence` == 100.00,     "", "x_"   )) %>%   mutate(node_hit = paste0(node, "__", exact_match, `Accession of closest sequence`)) %>%   mutate(marker.label = case_when(     `variation type` == "Inactivating mutation detected" ~ paste0(node_hit, ":-"),     !is.na(mutation) ~ paste0(node_hit, ":", mutation),     TRUE ~ node_hit   )) %>%   select(-obs_id)

title: Analysing clindamycin resistance in _Staphylococcus aureus_
text: Next, create binary matrix clindamycin resistance. can just markers variants markers. allow us see variants markers different associations clindamycin resistance.
code: # Generate binary matrix with markers cli_bin <- get_binary_matrix(   afp_CLI_public,   ast_CLI_public,   antibiotic = "Clindamycin",   drug_class_list = c("Lincosamides"),   sir_col = "pheno_eucast",   keep_assay_values = TRUE,   marker_col = "marker.label" ) ##  Converting ast_CLI_public column `drug_agent` to class `ab` in binary matrix ##  Some samples had multiple phenotype rows, taking the most resistant only for binary matrix ##  Defining NWT in binary matrix using ecoff column provided: ecoff  # Generate binary matrix with variants cli_bin_accession <- get_binary_matrix(   cli_accession,   ast_CLI_public,   antibiotic = "Clindamycin",   drug_class_list = c("Lincosamides"),   sir_col = "pheno_eucast",   keep_assay_values = TRUE,   marker_col = "marker.label" ) ##  Converting ast_CLI_public column `drug_agent` to class `ab` in binary matrix ##  Some samples had multiple phenotype rows, taking the most resistant only for binary matrix ##  Defining NWT in binary matrix using ecoff column provided: ecoff  # Visualize with UpSet plot (markers) cli_mic_upset <- amr_upset(   cli_bin,   min_set_size = 2,   assay = "mic",   order = "value",   print_set_size = TRUE,   plot_set_size = TRUE,   print_category_counts = TRUE,   bp_S = 0.25,   bp_R = 0.5 ) ##  Removing 687 rows with no phenotype call # Visualize with UpSet plot (variants) cli_mic_upset <- amr_upset(   cli_bin_accession,   min_set_size = 2,   assay = "mic",   order = "value",   print_set_size = TRUE,   plot_set_size = TRUE,   print_category_counts = TRUE,   bp_S = 0.25,   bp_R = 0.5 ) ##  Removing 687 rows with no phenotype call

title: Analysing clindamycin resistance in _Staphylococcus aureus_
text: Next, calculate solo positive predictive value (soloPPV) marker variant. allow us see markers variants predictive clindamycin resistance.   can clearly see analysis variants markers different associations clindamycin resistance. example, erm(C)_WP0012363.1 erm(C)_WP0012364.1 0.5 threshold. highlights importance considering specific variants markers predicting clindamycin resistance. Note: discrepancy may caused inducible resistance, always captured standard AST test. resistance gene may expressed conditions AST test, can induced presence certain antibiotics (e.g., erythromycin). known phenomenon clindamycin resistance S. aureus, presence erm gene can lead inducible resistance may detected standard AST test.
code: # soloPPV analysis for markers PPV_cli <- ppv(cli_bin, upset_grid = TRUE, plot_assay = TRUE) ##  Removing 687 rows with no phenotype call # soloPPV analysis for variants PPV_cli <- ppv(cli_bin_accession, upset_grid = TRUE, plot_assay = TRUE) ##  Removing 687 rows with no phenotype call

what: Assocation of specific variants with sequence types
title: Analysing clindamycin resistance in _Staphylococcus aureus_
text: Finally, can also look association specific marker variants sequence types, data available. can done creating binary matrix variants visualizing associations bubble plot.  can see last figure, erm(C)_WP0012364.1 quite rare, erm(C)_WP0012363.1 prevalent certain STs, ST22. suggests may clonal association variant ST, indicating resistance clone might inducible therefore difficult identify using standard AST.
code: # Identify "High-Frequency" STs because we have many STs with only a few samples, which can make the plot cluttered and less informative. By filtering to include only STs with a certain number of samples (e.g., n >= 100), we can focus on the most prevalent STs in the dataset, which are likely to provide more meaningful insights into the associations between specific variants and sequence types. high_freq_sts <- ST_data_CLI %>%   count(ST) %>%   filter(n >= 100) %>%   pull(ST)  # Merge with ST data cli_accession_st <- cli_accession %>%   inner_join(ST_data_CLI, by = "Name") %>%   filter(ST %in% high_freq_sts)  # Calculate prevalence of variant in each ST balloon_data <- cli_accession_st %>%   group_by(ST, marker.label) %>%   summarise(n_samples = n_distinct(Name), .groups = "drop") %>%   left_join(ST_data_CLI %>% count(ST, name = "total_st_n"), by = "ST") %>%   mutate(percent_prevalence = (n_samples / total_st_n) * 100) %>%   filter(percent_prevalence >= 10)  # Create the Balloon Plot showing only marker-ST combinations with at least 10% prevalence ggplot(balloon_data, aes(x = ST, y = marker.label)) +   geom_point(aes(size = n_samples, color = percent_prevalence)) +   scale_color_viridis_c(option = "viridis", direction = -1) +   scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 10)) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(     axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16, angle = 45, hjust = 1),     axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),     legend.title = element_text(size = 16),     legend.text = element_text(size = 14)   ) +   labs(     title = "Clindamycin Markers by Major Sequence Types",     subtitle = "Analysis of STs with n >= 100",     x = "Sequence Type (ST)",     y = "Marker variant",     size = "Sample Count",     color = "Prevalence (%)"   )

title: Genotype-Phenotype Integration for Antimicrobial Resistance Research
text: AMRgen open-source R package designed bridge gap genotypic phenotypic antimicrobial resistance (AMR) data. Developed extension AMR R package, provides tools interpret AMR genes, integrate findings antimicrobial susceptibility test (AST) data, calculate genotype-phenotype associations. package developed collaboration ESGEM-AMR Working Group tailored researchers healthcare professionals tackling AMR globally. AMRgen website full function documentation Vignette working analysing geno/pheno data using key functions.

title: Genotype-Phenotype Integration for Antimicrobial Resistance Research
text: Import Genotype Phenotype Data: Import common formats (NCBI EBI antibiogram format phenotypes; AMRFinderPlus hAMRonization genotypes Genotype-Phenotype Integration: Links AMR gene presence phenotypic resistance profiles, enabling deeper insights resistance mechanisms. Automated EUCAST MIC Distribution Integration: Fetch MIC distribution data directly EUCAST seamless comparison local susceptibility data. Visualisation: Generate powerful UpSet plots identify intersections AMR gene presence phenotypic resistance, highlighting multidrug resistance patterns. Modular Extensible: Leverages robust foundation AMR package, including antibiotic selectors clinical breakpoint interpretations. Planned development: NCBI-Compliant Export: Export phenotype data NCBI-compliant antibiogram format. Expanded Data Import: Import parse phenotype data tools (e.g. CARD, ResFinder).

title: Genotype-Phenotype Integration for Antimicrobial Resistance Research
code: library(AMRgen)

what: Investigate ciprofloxacin resistance vs quinolone genotype markers, via solo PPV and upset plots
title: Genotype-Phenotype Integration for Antimicrobial Resistance Research
code: # Example public E. coli AST data from NCBI #  (already imported via import_ncbi_ast() and re-interpreted with as.sir()) ecoli_ast  # Import matching E. coli AMRFinderPlus data from AllTheBacteria ecoli_geno <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name")  # Calculate solo positive predictive value for ciprofloxacin resistance, for individual markers found solo #  (for all quinolone-associated genotype markers) soloPPV_cipro <- solo_ppv_analysis(ecoli_geno, ecoli_ast, antibiotic="Ciprofloxacin", drug_class_list=c("Quinolones"), sir_col="pheno_clsi")  # Do upset plot of ciprofloxacin MIC vs quinolone genotype marker combinations #  (for combinations observed at least 5 times) cip_upset <- amr_upset(binary_matrix=soloPPV_cipro$amr_binary, min_set_size=5, assay="mic")  # Calculate positive predictive value for individual markers and combinations cip_ppv <- ppv(binary_matrix=soloPPV_cipro$amr_binary, min_set_size=5)  # Do logistic regression of ciprofloxacin resistance as a function of presence/absence of quinolone-associated markers #  (for markers observed at least 10 times) models <- amr_logistic(binary_matrix=soloPPV_cipro$amr_binary, maf=10)

what: Download reference MIC distribution from eucast.org and compare to example data
title: Genotype-Phenotype Integration for Antimicrobial Resistance Research
code: # Get MIC reference distribution for ciprofloxacin in E. coli ecoli_cip_mic_data <- get_eucast_mic_distribution("cipro", "E. coli")  # Plot the reference distribution  mics <- rep(ecoli_cip_mic_data$mic, ecoli_cip_mic_data$count) ggplot2::autoplot(mics, ab = "cipro", mo = "E. coli", title = "E. coli cipro reference distribution")  # Compare reference distribution to example E. coli data ecoli_cip <- ecoli_ast$mic[ecoli_ast$drug_agent=="CIP"] comparison <- compare_mic_with_eucast(ecoli_cip, ab = "cipro", mo = "E. coli") comparison ggplot2::autoplot(comparison)

what: Download phenotype or genotype data from EBI’s AMR portal
title: Genotype-Phenotype Integration for Antimicrobial Resistance Research
code: # Get phenotype data for ciprofloxacin in E. coli ebi_pheno_ecoli_cip <- download_ebi(species = "Escherichia coli", antibiotic="Cipro", reformat=T)  # Check how many observations we have for MIC data ebi_pheno_ecoli_cip %>% filter(!is.na(mic)) %>% nrow()  # Plot MIC distribution, stratified by platform assay_by_var(ebi_pheno_ecoli_cip, measure="mic", colour_by = "pheno_provided", facet_var = "platform")  # Compare MIC values with reference distribution from EUCAST compare_mics <- compare_mic_with_eucast(ebi_pheno_ecoli_cip$mic, ab = "cipro", mo = "E. coli") compare_mics ggplot2::autoplot(compare_mics)  # Compare disk diffusion zone values with reference distribution from EUCAST compare_disk <- compare_disk_with_eucast(ebi_pheno_ecoli_cip$disk, ab = "cipro", mo = "E. coli") compare_disk ggplot2::autoplot(compare_disk)  # Get genotype data for ciprofloxacin in E. coli ebi_geno <- download_ebi(data="genotype", species = "Escherichia coli", geno_subclass="QUINOLONE", reformat=T)  # Do upset plot of ciprofloxacin MIC vs quinolone genotype marker combinations #  (for combinations observed at least 5 times) cip_upset_ebi_mic <- amr_upset(geno_table=ecoli_geno, pheno_table=ebi_pheno_ecoli_cip, antibiotic="Ciprofloxacin", drug_class_list=c("Quinolones"), sir_col="pheno_provided", min_set_size=5, assay="mic", order="value")

what: Download phenotype data from NCBI BioSample
title: Genotype-Phenotype Integration for Antimicrobial Resistance Research
code: # Get all available phenotype data for Klebsiella quasipneumoniae # (note this is a small example with <100 samples, as this function queries NCBI BioSample via rentrez and is quite slow) ast <- download_ncbi_ast(   "Klebsiella quasipneumoniae",   reformat = TRUE )  # Plot MIC distribution, stratified by platform assay_by_var(ast, measure="mic", colour_by = "pheno_provided", facet_var = "platform")  # The downloaded S/I/R calls look odd, so re-interpret direct with latest EUCAST breakpoints and review ast <- interpret_ast(ast, interpret_eucast = T, interpret_clsi=T) assay_by_var(ast, measure="mic", colour_by = "pheno_eucast", facet_var = "platform") assay_by_var(ast, measure="mic", colour_by = "pheno_clsi", facet_var = "platform")

what: AMRgen: Genotype-Phenotype Integration for Antimicrobial Resistance Research — AMRgen-package
title: AMRgen: Genotype-Phenotype Integration for Antimicrobial Resistance Research — AMRgen-package
text: AMRgen package provides tools interpreting antimicrobial resistance (AMR) genes, integrating genomic data phenotypic antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data, calculating genotype-phenotype associations. package extends functionality AMR package, enabling researchers fetch MIC distribution data EUCAST, visualise multidrug resistance patterns using UpSet plots, generate NCBI-compliant antibiograms. Designed AMR research surveillance, AMRgen aims support data-driven decision-making combating AMR global scale.

title: ST data for Staphylococcus aureus genomes for clindamycin vignette — ST_data_CLI

ST: Sequence type, determined PubMLST scheme S. aureus. arc allele: Allele identifier arc locus PubMLST scheme. aroE allele: Allele identifier aroE locus PubMLST scheme. glpF allele: Allele identifier glpF locus PubMLST scheme. gmk allele: Allele identifier gmk locus PubMLST scheme. pta allele: Allele identifier pta locus PubMLST scheme. tpi allele: Allele identifier tpi locus PubMLST scheme. yqiL allele: Allele identifier yqiL locus PubMLST scheme.

title: AMR Logistic Regression Analysis — amr_logistic

title: AMR Logistic Regression Analysis — amr_logistic
code: amr_logistic(   geno_table,   pheno_table,   antibiotic = NULL,   drug_class_list = NULL,   geno_sample_col = NULL,   pheno_sample_col = NULL,   sir_col = "pheno",   ecoff_col = "ecoff",   marker_col = "marker.label",   binary_matrix = NULL,   maf = 10,   fit_glm = FALSE,   single_plot = TRUE,   colors = c("maroon", "blue4"),   axis_label_size = 9 )

title: AMR Logistic Regression Analysis — amr_logistic
text: geno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing genotype data, formatted import_amrfp(). used binary_matrix provided. pheno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing phenotype data, formatted import_ast(). used binary_matrix provided. antibiotic (Required binary_matrix provided) character string specifying antibiotic interest filter phenotype data. value must match one entries drug_agent column pheno_table. used binary_matrix provided breakpoints required. drug_class_list (Required binary_matrix provided) character vector drug classes filter genotype data markers related specified antibiotic. Markers geno_table filtered based whether drug_class matches value list. used binary_matrix provided. geno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name geno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. pheno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name pheno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. sir_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretation (SIR) data. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). provided, first column prefixed "phenotype*" used present, otherwise error thrown.  used binary_matrix provided. ecoff_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretations (SIR) made ECOFF rather clinical breakpoint. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). Default ecoff. Set NULL available.  used binary_matrix provided. marker_col (Optional) Name column containing marker identifiers, whose unique values treated predictors regression. Defaults "marker". binary_matrix data frame containing original binary matrix output get_binary_matrix() function. provided (set NULL), user must specify geno_table, pheno_table, antibiotic, drug_class_list optionally geno_sample_col, pheno_sample_col, sir_col, ecoff_col, marker_col pass get_binary_matrix(). maf (Optional) integer specifying minimum allele frequency (MAF) threshold. Markers MAF lower value excluded. Defaults 10. fit_glm (Optional) Change TRUE fit model glm. Otherwise fit model logistf (default FALSE). single_plot (Optional) logical value. TRUE, single plot produced comparing estimates resistance (R) non-resistance (NWT). Otherwise, two plots printed side--side. Defaults TRUE. colors (Optional) vector two colors, use R NWT models plots. Defaults c("maroon", "blue4"). axis_label_size (Optional) numeric value controlling size axis labels plot. Defaults 9.

title: AMR Logistic Regression Analysis — amr_logistic
text: list following components: binary_matrix: binary matrix genetic data phenotypic resistance information (either provided input generated function). modelR: fitted logistic regression model summary resistance (R). modelNWT: fitted logistic regression model summary non-resistance (NWT). raw_modelR: raw logistf glm model object resistance (R), suitable use predict(). raw_modelNWT: raw logistf glm model object non-resistance (NWT), suitable use predict(). plot: ggplot object comparing estimates resistance non-resistance corresponding statistical significance indicators.

title: AMR Logistic Regression Analysis — amr_logistic
code: # Example usage of the amr_logistic function result <- amr_logistic(   geno_table = import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name"),   pheno_table = ecoli_ast,   sir_col = "pheno_clsi",   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   drug_class_list = c("Quinolones"),   maf = 10 ) #> Generating geno-pheno binary matrix #>  Defining NWT in binary matrix using ecoff column provided: ecoff  #> ...Fitting logistic regression model to R using logistf #>    Filtered data contains 3629 samples (793 => 1, 2836 => 0) and 19 variables. #> Warning: logistf.fit: Maximum number of iterations for full model exceeded. Try to increase the number of iterations or alter step size by passing 'logistf.control(maxit=..., maxstep=...)' to parameter control #> ...Fitting logistic regression model to NWT using logistf #>    Filtered data contains 3576 samples (875 => 1, 2701 => 0) and 19 variables. #> Warning: logistf.fit: Maximum number of iterations for full model exceeded. Try to increase the number of iterations or alter step size by passing 'logistf.control(maxit=..., maxstep=...)' to parameter control #> Generating plots #> Plotting 2 models  # To access the plot: print(result$plot)

title: Generate Upset Plot — amr_upset
text: binary_matrix data frame containing original binary matrix output get_binary_matrix() function. provided (set NULL), user must specify geno_table, pheno_table, antibiotic, drug_class_list optionally geno_sample_col, pheno_sample_col, sir_col, ecoff_col, marker_col pass get_binary_matrix(). assay character string indicating whether plot MIC disk diffusion data. Must one : "mic": plot MIC data stored column mic "disk": plot disk diffusion data stored column disk min_set_size integer specifying minimum size gene set included analysis plots. Default 2. marker combinations least number occurrences included plots. order character string indicating order combinations x-axis. Options : "": decreasing frequency combinations "genes": order number genes combination "value" (default): order median assay value (MIC disk zone) combination "ppv": order PPV estimated combination geno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing genotype data, formatted import_amrfp(). used binary_matrix provided. pheno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing phenotype data, formatted import_ast(). used binary_matrix provided. antibiotic Optional. Antibiotic name used retrieve clinical breakpoints annotation assay distribution plot. drug_class_list (relevant binary_matrix provided) provided, AMR pkg used check class name/s associated antibiotic uses (printed screen user can see filtered). geno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name geno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. pheno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name pheno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. sir_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretation (SIR) data. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). provided, first column prefixed "phenotype*" used present, otherwise error thrown.  used binary_matrix provided. ecoff_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretations (SIR) made ECOFF rather clinical breakpoint. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). Default ecoff. Set NULL available.  used binary_matrix provided. marker_col character string specifying column name geno_table containing marker identifiers. Default "marker". used binary_matrix provided. plot_set_size Logical indicating whether include bar plot showing set size (.e., number times combination markers observed). Default FALSE. plot_category Logical indicating whether include stacked bar plot showing, marker combination, proportion samples phenotype classification (specified pheno column input file). Default TRUE. print_category_counts Logical indicating whether, plot_category=TRUE, print number strains resistance category marker combination plot. Default FALSE. print_set_size Logical indicating whether, plot_set_size=TRUE, print number strains marker combination plot. Default FALSE. boxplot_col Colour lines box plots summarising MIC distribution marker combination. Default "grey". SIR_col named vector colours percentage bar plot. names phenotype categories (e.g., "R", "", "S"), values valid color names hexadecimal color codes. Default values used AMR package AMR::scale_colour_sir(). species Optional. Species name used breakpoint lookup. bp_site Optional. Breakpoint site (e.g. "Non-meningitis") used retrieving clinical breakpoints. guideline Guideline used breakpoint lookup. Default "EUCAST 2025". bp_S (optional) S breakpoint add plot (numerical). bp_R (optional) R breakpoint add plot (numerical). ecoff_bp (optional) ECOFF breakpoint add plot (numerical).

title: Generate Upset Plot — amr_upset
text: list containing following elements: plot: grid plots displaying: () grid showing marker combinations observed, MIC distribution per marker combination, frequency per marker (optionally) phenotype classification /number samples marker combination. binary_matrix: copy genotype-phenotype binary matrix (either provided input generated function) summary: data frame summarizing marker combination observed, including median MIC (interquartile range), number resistant isolates, positive predictive value resistance.

title: Generate Upset Plot — amr_upset
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ecoli_geno <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name")  # Generate binary matrix binary_matrix <- get_binary_matrix(   geno_table = ecoli_geno,   pheno_table = ecoli_ast,   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   drug_class_list = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "pheno_clsi",   keep_assay_values = TRUE,   keep_assay_values_from = "mic" )  # Run upset plot analysis using this binary_matrix cip_mic_upset <- amr_upset(binary_matrix, assay = "mic")  # Alternatively, generate binary matrix and run ppv() in one step cip_mic_upset <- amr_upset(   assay = "mic",   geno_table = ecoli_geno,   pheno_table = ecoli_ast,   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   sir_col = "pheno_clsi" ) } # }

what: Generate a Stacked Bar Plot of Assay Values Colored by a Variable — assay_by_var
title: Generate a Stacked Bar Plot of Assay Values Colored by a Variable — assay_by_var
text: function creates stacked bar plot using ggplot2, x-axis represents MIC (Minimum Inhibitory Concentration) disk values, y-axis indicates frequency, bars colored variable (default, colours indicate whether assay value expressed range ). Plots can optionally faceted additional categorical variable. breakpoints provided, species drug provided can extract EUCAST breakpoints, vertical lines indicating S/R breakpoints ECOFF added plot.

title: Generate a Stacked Bar Plot of Assay Values Colored by a Variable — assay_by_var
code: assay_by_var(   pheno_table,   antibiotic = NULL,   measure = "mic",   colour_by = NULL,   bar_cols = NULL,   facet_var = NULL,   bp_site = NULL,   bp_S = NULL,   bp_R = NULL,   bp_ecoff = NULL,   species = NULL,   guideline = "EUCAST 2025",   bp_cols = c(S = "#3CAEA3", R = "#ED553B", E = "grey"),   x_axis_label = "Measurement",   y_axis_label = "Count",   colour_legend_label = NULL,   plot_title = NULL )

title: Generate a Stacked Bar Plot of Assay Values Colored by a Variable — assay_by_var
text: pheno_table Phenotype table standard format per import_ast(). antibiotic (optional) Name antibiotic filter 'drug_agent' column, retrieve breakpoints . measure Name column assay measurements plot (default "mic"). colour_by (optional) Field name containing variable colour bars (default NULL, colour bar indicate whether value expressed range ). bar_cols (optional) Manual colour scale use bar plot. NULL, colour_by variable class 'sir', bars default coloured using standard SIR colours. facet_var (optional) Column name containing variable facet (default NULL). bp_site (optional) Breakpoint site retrieve (relevant also supplying species antibiotic retrieve breakpoints, supplying breakpoints via bp_S, bp_R, ecoff). bp_S (optional) S breakpoint plot. bp_R (optional) R breakpoint plot. bp_ecoff (optional) ECOFF breakpoint plot. species (optional) Name species, can retrieve breakpoints print top plot help interpret . guideline (optional) Guideline use looking breakpoints (default 'EUCAST 2025'). bp_cols (optional) Manual colour scale breakpoint lines. x_axis_label (optional) String label x-axis (default "Measurement"). y_axis_label (optional) String label y-axis (default "Count"). colour_legend_label (optional) String label barplot fill colour legend (default NULL, results plotting variable name specified via 'colour_by' parameter). plot_title (optional) String title plot (default indicates whether MIC disk distribution plotted, prefixed antibiotic name provided, e.g. 'Ciprofloxacin MIC distribution')

title: Generate a Stacked Bar Plot of Assay Values Colored by a Variable — assay_by_var
text: stacked bar plot

title: Generate a Stacked Bar Plot of Assay Values Colored by a Variable — assay_by_var
code: # plot MIC distribution, highlighting values expressed as ranges assay_by_var(   pheno_table = ecoli_ast, antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   measure = "mic" )   # colour by SIR interpretation recorded in column 'pheno_clsi' assay_by_var(   pheno_table = ecoli_ast, antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   measure = "mic", colour_by = "pheno_clsi" )   # manually specify colours for the barplot assay_by_var(   pheno_table = ecoli_ast, antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   measure = "mic", colour_by = "pheno_clsi",   bar_cols = c(S = "skyblue", I = "orange", R = "maroon") )   # look up ECOFF and CLSI breakpoints and annotate these on the plot assay_by_var(   pheno_table = ecoli_ast, antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   measure = "mic", colour_by = "pheno_clsi",   species = "E. coli", guideline = "CLSI 2025" ) #>   MIC breakpoints determined using AMR package: S <= 0.25 and R > 1   # facet by method assay_by_var(   pheno_table = ecoli_ast, antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   measure = "mic", colour_by = "pheno_clsi",   species = "E. coli", guideline = "CLSI 2025",   facet_var = "method" ) #>   MIC breakpoints determined using AMR package: S <= 0.25 and R > 1

what: Check and Retrieve Breakpoints for an Antibiotic — checkBreakpoints
title: Check and Retrieve Breakpoints for an Antibiotic — checkBreakpoints
text: function checks clinical breakpoints specified antibiotic species using getBreakpoints function. handles cases multiple breakpoint sites exist uses specified site one highest susceptibility breakpoint.

title: Check and Retrieve Breakpoints for an Antibiotic — checkBreakpoints
code: checkBreakpoints(   species,   guide = "EUCAST 2024",   antibiotic,   assay = "MIC",   bp_site = NULL )

title: Check and Retrieve Breakpoints for an Antibiotic — checkBreakpoints
text: species character string representing species interest (e.g., "Escherichia coli"). guide character string indicating guideline breakpoints (default, "EUCAST 2024"). antibiotic character string indicating antibiotic check breakpoints (e.g., "Ciprofloxacin"). assay character string specifying assay type (either "MIC" "Disk"). Default "MIC". bp_site character string specifying breakpoint site use (optional). provided, function uses site; otherwise, different breakpoints specified different sites selects one highest susceptibility breakpoint.

title: Check and Retrieve Breakpoints for an Antibiotic — checkBreakpoints
text: list containing: breakpoint_S: susceptibility breakpoint (e.g., MIC disk size). breakpoint_R: resistance breakpoint (e.g., MIC disk size). bp_standard: breakpoint site used, multiple breakpoints set guidelines.

title: Check and Retrieve Breakpoints for an Antibiotic — checkBreakpoints
text: function first attempts retrieve breakpoints using getBreakpoints function. multiple breakpoint sites found, handles situation : Using specified site exists. Selecting breakpoint highest susceptibility value specified site found. Returning message selected site breakpoint values.

title: Check and Retrieve Breakpoints for an Antibiotic — checkBreakpoints
code: checkBreakpoints(   species = "Escherichia coli", guide = "EUCAST 2024",   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin", assay = "MIC" ) #>   MIC breakpoints determined using AMR package: S <= 0.25 and R > 0.5 #>   NOTE: Multiple breakpoint entries, for different sites: Non-meningitis; Meningitis. Using the one with the highest S breakpoint (Non-meningitis). #> $breakpoint_S #> [1] 0.25 #>  #> $breakpoint_R #> [1] 0.5 #>  #> $bp_standard #> [1] "Non-meningitis" #>

what: Generate a Series of Plots for AMR Gene and Combination Analysis — combo_stats

text: function generates set visualizations analyze AMR gene combinations, MIC values, gene prevalence input genotype-phenotype binary matrix. creates several plots, including assay distributions, phenotype breakdown, positive predictive values marker combination. amr_upset() ppv() functions can used generate standard data visualisations using component plots.

title: Generate a Series of Plots for AMR Gene and Combination Analysis — combo_stats
code: combo_stats(   binary_matrix,   min_set_size = 2,   order = "",   assay = "mic",   print_set_size = FALSE,   print_category_counts = FALSE,   SIR_col = c(S = "#3CAEA3", I = "#F6D55C", R = "#ED553B"),   boxplot_col = "grey",   colours_ppv = c(R = "maroon", NWT = "navy"),   antibiotic = NULL,   species = NULL,   bp_site = NULL,   guideline = "EUCAST 2025",   bp_S = NULL,   bp_R = NULL,   ecoff_bp = NULL,   pd = position_dodge(width = 0.8) )

title: Generate a Series of Plots for AMR Gene and Combination Analysis — combo_stats
text: binary_matrix data frame containing original binary matrix output get_binary_matrix() function. Expected columns identifier (column 1, name), pheno (class sir, S//R categories colour points), mic (class mic, MIC values plot), columns representing gene presence/absence (binary coded, .e., 1 = present, 0 = absent). min_set_size integer specifying minimum size gene set included analysis plots. Default 2. marker combinations least number occurrences included plots. order character string indicating order combinations x-axis. Options : "" (default): decreasing frequency combinations "genes": order number genes combination "value": order median assay value (MIC disk zone) combination "ppv": order PPV estimated combination assay character string indicating whether plot MIC disk diffusion data. Must one : "mic" (default): plot MIC data stored column mic "disk": plot disk diffusion data stored column disk NULL: plot summarise assay data print_set_size Logical indicating whether, plot_set_size=TRUE, print number strains marker combination plot. Default FALSE. print_category_counts Logical indicating whether, plot_category=TRUE, print number strains resistance category marker combination plot. Default FALSE. SIR_col named vector colours percentage bar plot. names phenotype categories (e.g., "R", "", "S"), values valid color names hexadecimal color codes. Default values used AMR package AMR::scale_colour_sir(). boxplot_col Colour lines box plots summarising MIC distribution marker combination. Default "grey". colours_ppv named vector colours plot PPV estimates. names "R", "" "NWT", values valid color names hexadecimal color codes. antibiotic Optional. Antibiotic name used retrieve clinical breakpoints annotation assay distribution plot. species Optional. Species name used breakpoint lookup. bp_site Optional. Breakpoint site (e.g. "Non-meningitis") used retrieving clinical breakpoints. guideline Guideline used breakpoint lookup. Default "EUCAST 2025". bp_S (optional) S breakpoint add plot (numerical). bp_R (optional) R breakpoint add plot (numerical). ecoff_bp (optional) ECOFF breakpoint add plot (numerical). pd ggplot2::position_dodge() object controlling horizontal spacing points confidence intervals PPV plot. Default position_dodge(width = 0.8).

title: Generate a Series of Plots for AMR Gene and Combination Analysis — combo_stats
text: list containing following elements: summary: data frame summarizing marker combination observed, including median MIC (interquartile range), number resistant isolates, positive predictive value resistance. assay_plot: MIC disk diffusion distribution per combination. setsize_plot: Bar plot showing number isolates per combination. marker_grid_plot: Dot plot showing gene combinations. marker_count_plot: Gene prevalence plot. category_plot: Stacked phenotype proportion plot. ppv_plot: Positive predictive value plot confidence intervals.

title: Generate a Series of Plots for AMR Gene and Combination Analysis — combo_stats
text: Marker combinations defined unique patterns gene presence/absence across isolates. combinations observed least min_set_size isolates included analysis. Median interquartile range calculated per combination. MIC data, medians may optionally exclude values expressed ranges (e.g. "<=0.25").

title: Generate a Series of Plots for AMR Gene and Combination Analysis — combo_stats
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ecoli_geno <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name")  binary_matrix <- get_binary_matrix(   geno_table = ecoli_geno,   pheno_table = ecoli_ast,   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   drug_class_list = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "pheno_clsi",   keep_assay_values = TRUE,   keep_assay_values_from = "mic" )  combo_matrix <- combo_stats(binary_matrix, min_set_size = 3, order = "value", assay = "mic") } # }

what: Plot to Compare Two Sets of Estimates — compare_estimates

title: Plot to Compare Two Sets of Estimates — compare_estimates
code: compare_estimates(   tbl1,   tbl2,   title1 = NULL,   title2 = NULL,   title = NULL,   sig = 0.05,   colors = c("maroon", "blue4"),   x_title = "Coefficient (95% CI)",   y_title = "Variant",   axis_label_size = 9,   single_plot = TRUE,   pd = position_dodge(width = 0.8),   marker_order = NULL )

title: Plot to Compare Two Sets of Estimates — compare_estimates
text: tbl1 tibble containing first set summary statistics (e.g., coefficients, p-values, CI) variant. Expected columns : marker: name marker (e.g., variable name). pval: p-value marker. ci.lower: lower bound confidence interval. ci.upper: upper bound confidence interval. est: estimated coefficient. tbl2 tibble containing second set summary statistics variant (format tbl1). title1 Title tbl1 data. single_plot, legend label tbl1 data; otherwise title tbl1 plot. title2 Title tbl2 data. single_plot, legend label tbl2 data; otherwise title tbl2 plot. title (optional) main title combined plot, single_plot TRUE. sig (optional) individual plots, p-value threshold data points highlight significant. Defaults 0.05. colors (optional) combined plot, vector two colors represent two input datasets. x_title (optional) title x-axis. Defaults "Coefficient (95% CI)". y_title (optional) title y-axis. Defaults "Variant". axis_label_size (optional) font size axis labels. Defaults 9. single_plot boolean indicating whether make single combined plot (TRUE), plot dataset side--side (FALSE). pd (optional) Position dodge, .e. spacing 2 estimates positioned /line. Default 'position_dodge(width = 0.8)' marker_order (optional) Vector indicating order markers plotted y-axis.

title: Plot to Compare Two Sets of Estimates — compare_estimates

what: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
text: Compares genotypes (presence resistance markers) observed phenotypes (resistant vs susceptible) using binary matrix get_binary_matrix(). genotypic prediction variable defined basis presence genotype markers (either marker input table, defined input inclusion list exclusion list). genotypic prediction compared observed phenotypes using standard classification metrics (via yardstick pkg) AMR-specific error rates (major error, major error, VME) per ISO 20776-2 (see FDA definitions). Supports evaluating R NWT outcomes single call, flexible prediction rules marker inclusion options.

title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
code: concordance(   binary_matrix,   markers = NULL,   exclude_markers = NULL,   ppv_threshold = NULL,   ppv_results = NULL,   truth = c("R", "NWT"),   prediction_rule = "any",   min_count = NULL,   logreg_results = NULL,   pval_threshold = NULL )

title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
text: binary_matrix data frame output get_binary_matrix(), containing one row per sample, columns indicating binary phenotypes (R, , NWT) binary marker presence/absence. markers character vector marker column names include new binary outcome variable genotypic_prediction. Default NULL includes marker columns. exclude_markers character vector marker column names exclude genotypic prediction. Applied markers filtering. ppv_threshold numeric PPV threshold (0-1). Markers solo PPV value excluded. Requires ppv_results. ppv_results Output solo_ppv_analysis() ppv(), used solo PPV-based marker filtering ppv_threshold set. output ppv() supplied, single-marker entries (marker_count == 1) summary table used obtain per-marker PPVs, making behaviour equivalent supplying solo_ppv_analysis() output directly. truth character vector specifying phenotypic truth column(s) evaluate: "R" (resistant vs susceptible/intermediate), "NWT" (non-wildtype vs wildtype), c("R", "NWT") (default) evaluate . prediction_rule rule generating genotypic predictions: "" (default) predicts positive marker present (applying filters specified markers, exclude_markers, ppv_threshold, pval_threshold, min_count); "" predicts positive markers present (applying filters specified markers, exclude_markers, ppv_threshold, pval_threshold, min_count); positive integer predicts positive least many markers present (applying filters specified markers, exclude_markers, ppv_threshold, pval_threshold, min_count); "logistic" uses logistic regression model logreg_results predict outcomes sample. min_count integer NULL. Exclude markers total frequency (column sum binary_matrix) value. Default NULL (filtering). logreg_results Output amr_logistic(). Used p-value filtering (pval_threshold set) prediction_rule = "logistic". pval_threshold numeric p-value threshold. Exclude markers logistic regression p-value >= value. Requires logreg_results.

title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
text: S3 object class "amr_concordance", list containing: conf_mat: Named list yardstick confusion matrix objects (e.g. list(R = <cm>, NWT = <cm>)). metrics: tibble columns outcome, metric, estimate. data: input binary matrix added R_pred /NWT_pred columns. markers_used: Named list character vectors markers used per outcome. truth_col: Character vector truth columns evaluated. n: Named integer vector sample counts per outcome. prediction_rule: prediction rule used.

title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
text: function identifies marker columns columns reserved set (id, pheno, ecoff, R, , NWT, mic, disk). applies filtering order: inclusion list markers, exclusion list exclude_markers, min_count, ppv_threshold filtering, pval_threshold filtering. Marker filtering performed per outcome (R, NWT), since PPV-based p-value-based filters category/outcome-specific. Standard metrics (sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, accuracy, kappa, F-measure) calculated using pkg yardstick. AMR-specific error rates computed internally: VME (Major Error): FN / (TP + FN) = 1 - sensitivity. Proportion truly resistant isolates predicted genotype. (Major Error): FP / (TN + FP) = 1 - specificity. Proportion truly susceptible isolates incorrectly predicted resistant genotype.

title: Calculate genotype-phenotype concordance from binary matrix — concordance
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ geno_table <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name")  binary_matrix <- get_binary_matrix(   geno_table = geno_table,   pheno_table = ecoli_ast,   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   drug_class_list = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "pheno_clsi" )  # Basic concordance for both R and NWT result <- concordance(binary_matrix) result  # Single outcome result <- concordance(binary_matrix, truth = "R")  # Exclude specific markers result <- concordance(binary_matrix, exclude_markers = c("qnrS1"))  # Filter markers by solo PPV threshold, using solo_ppv_analysis() output solo_ppv <- solo_ppv_analysis(binary_matrix = binary_matrix) result <- concordance(   binary_matrix,   ppv_threshold = 0.5,   ppv_results = solo_ppv )  # Filter markers by solo PPV threshold, using ppv() output ppv_res <- ppv(binary_matrix = binary_matrix) result <- concordance(   binary_matrix,   ppv_threshold = 0.5,   ppv_results = ppv_res )  # Require at least 2 markers present for prediction result <- concordance(binary_matrix, prediction_rule = 2)  # Use logistic regression model for prediction logreg <- amr_logistic(binary_matrix = binary_matrix) result <- concordance(   binary_matrix,   prediction_rule = "logistic",   logreg_results = logreg )  # Access components result$conf_mat$R result$metrics result$markers_used } # }

what: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
title: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
text: function retrieve genotype phenotype data EBI AMR Portal FTP site. portal uses AMRfinderplus identify AMR-associated genotypes, results processed fields returned AMRfinderplus included. Optionally, function can also reformat phenotype data easy use AMRgen functions (using import_ebi_ast_ftp) re-interpret assay measures using latest breakpoints/ECOFF.

title: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
code: download_ebi(   data = "phenotype",   antibiotic = NULL,   force_antibiotic = FALSE,   genus = NULL,   species = NULL,   geno_subclass = NULL,   geno_class = NULL,   remove_dup = FALSE,   release = NULL,   reformat = FALSE,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
text: data String specifying type data download, either "phenotype" "genotype" (default "phenotype"). antibiotic (Optional) String (vector strings) specifying antibiotic name/s filter (default NULL). Uses AMR package try fix typos, format lower-case EBI files. used data="genotype" class subclass specified. force_antibiotic (Optional) Logical indicating whether turn parsing antibiotic names match exactly input strings (default FALSE). genus (Optional) String specifying bacterial genus filter (default NULL, pull taxa). species (Optional) String specifying bacterial species filter (default NULL, pull taxa). used genus specified. geno_subclass (Optional) String specifying antibiotic subclass filter genotype data (default NULL). Filter based string match, identity, e.g. subclass="TRIMETHOPRIM" return rows string "TRIMETHOPRIM" included subclass field. used data="genotype". Check NCBI AMR Class-Subclass Reference valid terms. geno_class (Optional) String specifying antibiotic subclass filter genotype data (default NULL). Filter based string match, identity, e.g. class="TRIMETHOPRIM" return rows string "TRIMETHOPRIM" included class field. used data="genotype" subclass specified. Check NCBI AMR Class-Subclass Reference valid terms. remove_dup (Optional) Logical specifying whether clean genotype data removing duplicates hit (default FALSE). detected gene associated subclass value actually list multiple drugs/classes, e.g. subclass="GENTAMICIN/TOBRAMYCIN", EBI data table duplicate rows gene hit, different values antibiotic_name associated antibiotic_ontology antibiotic_ontology_link annotation fields (e.g. one row gentamicin tobramycin). remove duplicate rows (drug-specific annotation fields) return one row per hit (.e. restoring AMRfinderplus output format), set TRUE. release (Optional) String specifying data release download (default NULL, pull latest release). reformat (Optional) Logical specifying whether reformat downloaded data easy use downstream AMRgen functions, using import_ebi_ast_ftp (phenotypes) import_amrfp_ebi_ftp. Default FALSE. things like format antibiotic, measurement, phenotype columns AMR package classes. set TRUE can also turn re-interpreting MIC/disk data using latest EUCAST/CLSI breakpoints (data="phenotype"). columns removed downloaded data frame, key fields renamed, see documentation format_ast import_amrfp_ebi_ftp. interpret_eucast (Optional) Logical specifying whether re-interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class sir. used downloading phenotype data, reformat set TRUE. interpret_clsi (Optional) Logical specifying whether re-interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class sir. used downloading phenotype data, reformat set TRUE. interpret_ecoff (Optional) Logical specifying whether re-interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class sir coded R (nonwildtype) S (wildtype). used downloading phenotype data, reformat set TRUE.

title: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
text: data frame containing phenotype genotype data retrieved EBI, optionally reformatted AMRgen standard formats classes.

title: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
text: See https://www.ebi.ac.uk/amr// information available portal, https://github.com/ncbi/amr/wiki/class-subclass valid class subclass terms. Note function downloads full genotype phenotype data table filtering provided parameters, trouble drug/class names matching just run without specifying genus/species/antibiotic/class filters, get full unfiltered table explore field values filter manually get want.

title: Download antimicrobial genotype or phenotype data from the EBI AMR Portal — download_ebi
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # download all phenotype data from EBI pheno_ebi <- download_ebi()  # download phenotype data from Dec 2025 release, and filter to Salmonella pheno_salmonella <- download_ebi(   genus = "Salmonella",   release = "2025-12" )  # reformat downloaded phenotype data to simplify use with AMRgen functions pheno_salmonella <- import_ebi_ast_ftp(pheno_salmonella)   # download phenotype data for Salmonella, filter to ampicillin and ciprofloxacin pheno_salmonella <- download_ebi(   genus = "Salmonella",   antibiotic = c("ampicillin", "Cipro") )  # download phenotype data for Staphylococcus aureus and reformat # for use with AMRgen functions pheno_staph <- download_ebi(   species = "Staphylococcus aureus",   reformat = T )  # download phenotype data for Klebsiella quasipneumoniae, reformat # for use with AMRgen functions, and re-interpret phenotypes pheno_kquasi_reinterpreted <- download_ebi(   species = "Klebsiella quasipneumoniae",   reformat = T,   interpret_eucast = TRUE,   interpret_clsi = TRUE,   interpret_ecoff = TRUE )  # download all available genotype data ebi_geno <- download_ebi(data = "genotype")  # download genotype data for Klebsiella pneumoniae, and filter to # markers assigned to NCBI class 'TRIMETHOPRIM' geno_kpn_tmp <- download_ebi(   data = "genotype",   species = "Klebsiella pneumoniae",   geno_subclass = "TRIMETHOPRIM" )  # download genotype data for Klebsiella pneumoniae, and filter to # markers assigned to NCBI class 'TRIMETHOPRIM' or 'QUINOLONE' geno_kpn_tmp <- download_ebi(   data = "genotype",   species = "Klebsiella pneumoniae",   geno_subclass = c("TRIMETHOPRIM", "QUINOLONE") )  # download genotype data for Klebsiella pneumoniae, and filter to # markers associated with CARD drug term 'trimethoprim' geno_kpn_tmp <- download_ebi(   data = "genotype",   species = "Klebsiella pneumoniae",   antibiotic = "trimethoprim" ) } # }

what: Download NCBI antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data — download_ncbi_ast
title: Download NCBI antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data — download_ncbi_ast
text: function downloads antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data NCBI Pathogen Detection database via BioSample API. Data retrieved batches, parsed XML, returned tidy tibble metadata including BioSample ID, Bioproject ID, organism name.

title: Download NCBI antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data — download_ncbi_ast
code: download_ncbi_ast(   species,   antibiotic = NULL,   max_records = 15000,   batch_size = 200,   sleep_time = 0.34,   force_antibiotic = FALSE,   reformat = FALSE,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Download NCBI antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data — download_ncbi_ast
text: species Character. Organism name search query (e.g., "Salmonella enterica"). Required. antibiotic Character vector. Optional antibiotic name/s filter returned data. Strings processed using AMR package standardize names matching, e.g. "amikacin" "Amikacin" "ami" parsed "amikacin" matching. can turned setting force_antibiotic=TRUE. Full list allowed antibiotic names NCBI: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/biosample/docs/antibiogram/. max_records Integer. Maximum number BioSample records retrieve. Default 15000. batch_size Integer. Number records fetched per API request. Default 200 recommended NCBI. sleep_time Numeric. Seconds pause batch requests avoid overloading NCBI servers. Default 0.34. force_antibiotic Logical. TRUE, turns standardizing antibiotic name using AMR package filtering, matching done exactly input string/s. Default FALSE. reformat Logical. TRUE, reformats output using import_ncbi_biosample compatibility AMR analysis workflows. Default FALSE. set TRUE, data can also interpreted breakpoints/ECOFF setting interpret_*=TRUE. interpret_eucast Logical. Passed interpret_ast via import_ncbi_biosample. TRUE, interprets MIC values using EUCAST breakpoints. Default FALSE. used reformat=TRUE. interpret_clsi Logical. Passed interpret_ast via import_ncbi_biosample. TRUE, interprets MIC values using CLSI breakpoints. Default FALSE. used reformat=TRUE. interpret_ecoff Logical. Passed interpret_ast via import_ncbi_biosample. TRUE, interprets MIC values using ECOFF cutoffs. Default FALSE. used reformat=TRUE.

title: Download NCBI antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data — download_ncbi_ast
text: tibble one row per AST measure, corresponding BioSample metadata.

title: Download NCBI antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data — download_ncbi_ast
text: function constructs Entrez query form: "<organism> antibiogram[filter]". XML records downloaded batches, parsed, combined single table. resulting tibble contains AST test results associated metadata including: id: BioSample identifier BioProject: BioProject accession ID organism: Organism name Antibiotic, Phenotype, Measurement, Units, Method, System, Manufacturer, Panel, Standard: AST data columns function can optionally filter one antibiotics. can also optionally reformat data compatibility AMRgen functions via import_ncbi_ast, interpret raw data measures breakpoints ECOFF. See import_ncbi_ast details output formats options used.

title: Download NCBI antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data — download_ncbi_ast
text: Users encouraged set NCBI API key via rentrez::set_entrez_key() increase request limits comply NCBI usage policies.

text: eurogasp_geno_raw data frame 54,764 rows 24 columns: Name: Sample identifier (ENA run accession). Protein identifier: Protein identifier (used N. gonorrhoeae assemblies; NA). Contig id: Assembly contig identifier. Start, Stop: Start stop positions AMR element contig. Strand: Strand orientation (+ -). Element symbol: AMRFinderPlus element symbol (gene name /mutation, e.g. gyrA_S91F). Sequence name: Full descriptive name AMR element. Scope: AMRFinderPlus scope (core plus). Element type: Type AMR element (e.g. AMR). Element subtype: Subtype AMR element (e.g. POINT, AMR). Class: Antibiotic class (e.g. QUINOLONE, BETA-LACTAM). Subclass: Antibiotic subclass (e.g. CEPHALOSPORIN, TETRACYCLINE). Method: Detection method used AMRFinderPlus (e.g. POINTX, BLASTX). Target length, Reference sequence length, Alignment length: Sequence length metrics (bp). % Coverage reference sequence: Percentage reference sequence covered alignment. % Identity reference sequence: Percentage identity closest reference sequence. Accession closest sequence: NCBI accession closest reference sequence. Name closest sequence: Name closest reference sequence. HMM id, HMM description: HMM-based annotation fields (NA dataset). Hierarchy node: Gene hierarchy node name, required import_amrfp (enabled --print_node flag).

Default "pheno_provided". breakpoint_version Character string specifying breakpoint version used interpretation (e.g. "EUCAST 2024"). submission_account Character string specifying EBI Webin submission account identifier (e.g. "Webin-###"). provided, JSON output files generated function return formated table , can updated converted submission-ready JSON later using format_ebi_json(). domain Character string specifying domain used submission metadata (e.g. "self.ExampleDomain"). provided, JSON output files generated function return formated table , can updated converted submission-ready JSON later using format_ebi_json(). output_dir Character string specifying directory JSON files written. provided, JSON output files generated function return formated table , can updated converted submission-ready JSON later using format_ebi_json().
