what: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from a generic format — format_ast
title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from a generic format — format_ast
text: function attempts import antibiotic susceptibility testing (AST) data long-form antibiogram format (one row per sample test), suitable downstream use AMRgen analysis functions. assumes input file tab-delimited text file (e.g., TSV) CSV (may compressed) parses relevant columns (antibiotic names, species names, MIC disk data, S//R calls) suitable classes using AMR package. optionally can use AMR package interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) based EUCAST CLSI guidelines (human breakpoints /ECOFF). expected columns found warnings given, interpretation may possible.

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from a generic format — format_ast
code: format_ast(   input,   sample_col = "id",   species = NULL,   species_col = "spp_pheno",   ab = NULL,   ab_col = "drug_agent",   mic_col = "mic",   disk_col = "disk",   pheno_cols = c("ecoff", "pheno_eucast", "pheno_clsi", "pheno_provided"),   method_col = "method",   platform_col = "platform",   source_col = "source",   guideline_col = "guideline",   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE,   rename_cols = TRUE )

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from a generic format — format_ast
text: input string representing dataframe, path input file, containing AST data long-form antibiogram format (one row per sample test). might file containing content EBI/NCBI AST dataset previously processed using import_ebi_ast(), import_ncbi_ast(), import_ast() functions, files similar format/structure different column names. sample_col (optional, default 'id') Name input data column provides sample name. 'rename' parameter set TRUE, column renamed 'id'. species (optional) Name single species samples belong. Use want interpret assay measurements input file contain column indicating species sample (called 'species' name specified species_col parameter). species_col (optional, default 'species') Name input data column provides species name. provided, column converted micro-organism class 'mo' via AMR::.mo(). 'rename' parameter set TRUE, column also renamed 'spp_pheno'. interpretation switched , column used identify appropriate  breakpoints interpretation row data table. ab (optional) Name single antibiotic use phenotype interpretation. Use want interpret assay measurements input file contain column indicating drug sample (called 'drug_agent' name specified ab_col parameter). ab_col (optional, default 'drug_agent') Name input data column provides drug name. provided, column converted antibiotic class 'ab' via AMR::.ab(). 'rename' parameter set TRUE, column also renamed 'drug_agent'. interpretation switched , column used identify appropriate breakpoints interpretation row data table. mic_col (optional, default 'mic') Name input data column provides MIC measurements. provided, column converted MIC class 'mic' via AMR::.mic(). 'rename' parameter set TRUE, column also renamed 'mic'. interpretation switched , MIC values interpreted clinical breakpoints. disk_col (optional, default 'disk') Name input data column provides disk diffusion zone measurements. provided, column converted disk diffusion class 'disk' via AMR::.disk(). 'rename' parameter set TRUE, column also renamed 'disk'. interpretation switched , zone values interpreted clinical breakpoints. pheno_cols (optional, default c("ecoff", "pheno_eucast", "pheno_clsi", "pheno_provided")) Name input data column/s provides disk diffusion zone measurements (character vector, single string single column). provided, columns converted SIR class 'sir' via AMR::.sir(). method_col (optional, default 'method') Name input data column indicates testing method used (e.g. broth dilution, disk diffusion). 'rename' parameter set TRUE, column also renamed 'method'. platform_col (optional, default 'platform') Name input data column indicates testing platform used (e.g. Vitek, Sensititre). 'rename' parameter set TRUE, column also renamed 'platform'. source_col (optional, default 'source') Name input data column indicates source dataset (e.g. BioProject, PMID). 'rename' parameter set TRUE, column also renamed 'source'. guideline_col (optional, default 'guideline') Name input data column indicates guideline used testing (e.g. EUCAST, CLSI). 'rename' parameter set TRUE, column also renamed 'guideline'. interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class 'sir'. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class 'sir'. interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class 'sir' coded 'R' (nonwildtype) 'S' (wildtype). rename_cols logical value (default TRUE). TRUE, function rename provided columns (specified ab_col, mic_col, disk_col, species_col, id_col) default names expected AMRgen functions ('drug_agent', 'mic', 'disk', 'spp_pheno', 'id'), match output import_ast() functions.

what: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
text: function generates binary matrix representing resistance (R vs S/) nonwildtype (NWT vs WT, R/vs S) status given antibiotic, presence absence genetic markers related one specified drug classes. takes input separate tables genotype phenotype data, matches according common identifier (either specified column names assuming first column contains ID), filters data according specified antibiotic drug class criteria creating binary matrix. Suitable input files can generated using import_ast() import phenotype data, import_amrfp() import genotype data AMRFinderPlus.

title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
code: get_binary_matrix(   geno_table,   pheno_table,   antibiotic,   drug_class_list = NULL,   keep_SIR = TRUE,   keep_assay_values = FALSE,   keep_assay_values_from = c("mic", "disk"),   geno_sample_col = NULL,   pheno_sample_col = NULL,   sir_col = "pheno_clsi",   ecoff_col = "ecoff",   marker_col = "marker",   most_resistant = TRUE )

title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
text: geno_table data frame containing genotype data, long form one row per sample genetic marker. Expected format output import_amrfp() must include column labeled drug_class (indicating antibiotic class associated marker), addition column indicating marker (column name specified via marker_col) column sample identifiers (specified via geno_sample_col, otherwise assumed first column contains identifiers). pheno_table data frame containing phenotype data, long form one row per sample, drug assay result. Expected format output import_ast() must include column drug_agent (indicating drug agent, interpretable AMR pkg class ab), addition column sample identifiers (specified via pheno_sample_col, otherwise assumed first column contains identifiers), column resistance interpretation (S//R, specified via sir_col), optionally column ECOFF interpretation (WT/NWT S/R, specified via ecoff_col). antibiotic character string specifying antibiotic interest filter phenotype data. value must match one entries drug_agent column pheno_table coercible match using AMR::.ab. drug_class_list character vector (optional) drug classes filter genotype data markers related specified antibiotic. Markers geno_table filtered based whether drug_class matches value list. provided, AMR pkg used check class name/s associated antibiotic uses (printed screen user can see filtered). keep_SIR logical indicating whether retain full S//R phenotype column output. Defaults TRUE. keep_assay_values logical indicating whether include columns raw phenotype assay data. Assumes columns labelled "mic" "disk"; added output table present. Defaults FALSE. keep_assay_values_from character vector specifying assay values (e.g., "mic", "disk") retain keep_assay_values TRUE. Defaults c("mic", "disk"). geno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name geno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. pheno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name pheno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. sir_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretation (SIR) data. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). provided, first column prefixed "phenotype*" used present, otherwise error thrown.  used binary_matrix provided. ecoff_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains ECOFF interpretation phenotype. values interpretable "WT" (wildtype) "NWT" (nonwildtype), "S" / "" / "R". Default "ecoff. marker_col character string specifying column name geno_table containing marker identifiers. Defaults "marker". most_resistant logical indicating whether, multiple phenotype entries present sample drug, resistant kept (otherwise least resistant kept). Default TRUE.

title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
text: data frame row represents sample, column represents genetic marker related specified antibiotic's drug class. binary values matrix indicate presence (1) absence (0) marker sample, along resistance status columns specified antibiotic: R resistant (defined sir_col, 1=R, 0=/S) NWT nonwildtype (defined ecoff_col provided: 1=NWT, 0=WT; otherwise defined sir_col: 1=/R, 0=S).

title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
text: function performs several steps: Verifies pheno_table contains drug_agent column converts class ab necessary. Filters pheno_table retain data related specified antibiotic. Checks geno_table contains markers associated specified drug class(es). Matches sample identifiers geno_table pheno_table. Extracts transforms phenotype data binary format indicating resistance NWT status. Constructs binary matrix genotype data, column representing genetic marker. Returns single matrix sample identifiers plus binary variables phenotype genetic marker.

title: Get Binary Matrix of Genotype and Phenotype Data — get_binary_matrix
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Import example E. coli AMRFinderPlus data from AllTheBacteria ecoli_geno <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name") geno_pheno_cip <- get_binary_matrix(   ecoli_geno,   ecoli_ast,   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   drug_class_list = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "pheno_clsi" ) geno_pheno_cip <- get_binary_matrix(   ecoli_geno,   ecoli_ast,   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   drug_class_list = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "Resistance phenotype",   keep_assay_values = TRUE ) } # }

title: Extract Details from a Generalized Linear Model — glm_details
code: # Generate example data set.seed(1) dat <- data.frame(   R = rbinom(100, 1, 0.3),   geneA = rbinom(100, 1, 0.2),   geneB = rbinom(100, 1, 0.5),   geneC = rbinom(100, 1, 0.3) )  # Fit logistic regression model and extract details model <- glm(R ~ ., data = dat, family = binomial(link = "logit")) model_details <- glm_details(model) #> Waiting for profiling to be done...  # Plot model summary ggplot2::autoplot(model_details)

what: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp
title: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp
text: function imports processes AMRFinderPlus results, extracting antimicrobial resistance (AMR) elements mapping standardised antibiotic names drug classes. function also converts gene symbols harmonised format ensures compatibility AMR package.

title: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp
code: import_amrfp(   input_table,   sample_col = "Name",   element_symbol_col = NULL,   element_type_col = NULL,   element_subtype_col = "Element subtype",   method_col = "Method",   node_col = "Hierarchy node",   subclass_col = "Subclass",   class_col = "Class",   amrfp_drugs = amrfp_drugs_table )

title: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp
text: input_table character string specifying dataframe path AMRFinderPlus results table (TSV format). sample_col character string specifying column identifies samples dataset (default Name). element_symbol_col Optional character string specifying column containing gene element symbols non-standard column names used. element_type_col Optional character string specifying column indicating element type (e.g. AMR). element_subtype_col Character string specifying column used detect mutation subtypes. method_col Character string specifying AMRFinderPlus method column. node_col Character string specifying hierarchy node column. subclass_col Character string specifying AMRFinderPlus subclass column. class_col Character string specifying AMRFinderPlus class column. amrfp_drugs tibble containing reference table mapping AMRFinderPlus subclasses (AMRFP_Subclass) standardised drug classes (drug_class). Defaults amrfp_drugs_table, provided internally.

title: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp
text: tibble containing processed AMR elements, harmonised gene names, mapped drug agents, drug classes. output retains original columns AMRFinderPlus table along newly mapped variables.

title: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp
text: function performs following steps: Reads AMRFinderPlus output table. Filters data include AMR elements. Converts gene symbols harmonised format. Splits multiple subclass annotations separate rows. Maps AMRFinderPlus subclasses standardised drug class names using amrfp_drugs. Converts drug agent names "ab" class AMR package. processing ensures compatibility downstream AMR analysis workflows.

title: Import and Process AMRFinderPlus Results — import_amrfp
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # small example E. coli AMRFinderPlus data data(ecoli_geno_raw) ecoli_geno_raw  # import first few rows of this data frame and parse it as AMRfp data geno <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw %>% head(n = 10), "Name") geno } # }

title: Import EBI-processed AMRFinderPlus Genotypes — import_amrfp_ebi
text: input_table R object file path input EBI genotype table (R object, file path TSV CSV file). web Logical indicating whether data web portal (default FALSE). FALSE input assumed FTP download_ebi.

title: Import EBI-processed AMRFinderPlus Genotypes — import_amrfp_ebi
text: tibble containing processed AMR elements, harmonised gene names, drug agents, drug classes. output retains original columns AMRFinderPlus table along newly mapped variables.

title: Import EBI-processed AMRFinderPlus Genotypes — import_amrfp_ebi
text: data pre-processed EBI match NCBI class/subclass CARD's antibiotic resistance ontology (ARO), however consistency function re-process data generate drug_agent drug_class fields consistent import_amrfp() function (EBI fields antibiotic* also retained). Note several AMRfinderplus fields excluded EBI files, including hierarchy node, method, percent identity coverage; therefore unlike import_amrfp() function, function assign variation type node. function performs following steps: Reads EBI-processed genotype table. Maps AMRFinderPlus subclasses standardised drug agent drug class names using amrfp_drugs (EBI-mappings retained antibiotic* fields.) Converts drug agent names ab class AMR package. processing ensures compatibility downstream AMR analysis workflows.

title: Import EBI-processed AMRFinderPlus Genotypes — import_amrfp_ebi

what: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from common sources — import_ast
title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from common sources — import_ast
text: function imports antibiotic susceptibility testing (AST) datasets formats exported EBI, NCBI, WHOnet several automated AST instruments (Vitek, Microscan, Sensititre). assumes input file tab-delimited text file (e.g., TSV) CSV (may compressed) parses relevant columns (antibiotic names, species names, MIC disk data) suitable classes using AMR package. optionally can use AMR package interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) based EUCAST CLSI guidelines (human breakpoints /ECOFF). expected columns found warnings given, interpretation may possible.

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from common sources — import_ast
code: import_ast(   input,   format = "ebi",   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE,   species = NULL,   ab = NULL,   source = NULL )

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from common sources — import_ast
text: input string representing dataframe, path input file, containing AST data supported format. files may downloaded public sources EBI AMR web browser (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/amr/data/?view=experiments), EBI FTP site (ftp://ftp.ebi.ac.uk/pub/databases/amr_portal/releases/), NCBI browser (e.g. https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pathogens/ast#Pseudomonas%20aeruginosa), using functions download_ebi download_ncbi_ast; files may exported supported AST instruments. format string indicating format data: "ebi" (default), "ebi_web", "ebi_ftp", "ncbi", "vitek", "microscan", "sensititre", "whonet". determines whether data passed import_ebi_ast() (ebi/ebi_web), import_ebi_ast_ftp() (ebi_ftp), import_ncbi_ast() (ncbi), import_vitek_ast() (vitek), import_microscan_ast() (microscan), import_sensititre_ast() (sensititre), import_whonet_ast() (whonet) function process. interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class 'sir'. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class 'sir'. interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class 'sir' coded 'R' (nonwildtype) 'S' (wildtype). species (optional) Name species use phenotype interpretation. default, organism field input file assumed specify species sample, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single species name via parameter. ab (optional) Name antibiotic use phenotype interpretation. default, antibiotic field input file assumed specify antibiotic sample, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single antibiotic name via parameter. source (optional) single value record source data points, e.g. "EBI_browser". default, publications field (EBI data) BioProject field (NCBI data) used indicate source row input file, missing want override single value samples, may provide source name via parameter.

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data from common sources — import_ast
text: data frame processed AST data, including additional columns: id: biosample identifier. spp_pheno: species phenotype, formatted using .mo function. drug_agent: antibiotic used test, formatted using .ab function. mic: minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) value, formatted using .mic function. disk: disk diffusion measurement (mm), formatted using .disk function. method: AST method (e.g., "broth dilution", "disk diffusion", "Etest", "agar dilution"). Expected values based NCBI/EBI antibiogram specification. platform: AST platform/instrument (e.g., "Vitek", "Phoenix", "Sensititre"). guideline: AST standard recorded input file used AST assay. pheno_eucast: phenotype newly interpreted EUCAST human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_clsi: phenotype newly interpreted CLSI human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. ecoff: phenotype newly interpreted ECOFF (S/R), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_provided: original phenotype interpretation provided input file. source: source data point (publications bioproject field input file, replaced single value passed 'source' parameter).

what: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_ast
title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_ast
text: function imports antibiotic susceptibility testing (AST) dataset downloaded EBI AMR portal website (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/amr/data/?view=experiments) Data downloaded EBI AMR Portal FTP site (ftp://ftp.ebi.ac.uk/pub/databases/amr_portal/releases/), either directly via function download_ebi(), formatted differently can instead processed using import_ebi_ast_ftp() function.

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_ast
code: import_ebi_ast(   input,   sample_col = "phenotype-BioSample_ID",   source = NULL,   species = NULL,   ab = NULL,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_ast
text: input string representing dataframe, path input file, containing AST data EBI antibiogram format. files can downloaded EBI AMR browser, e.g. https://www.ebi.ac.uk/amr/data/?view=experiments sample_col string indicating name column sample identifiers. NULL, assume 'phenotype-BioSample_ID'. source (optional) single value record source data points, e.g. "EBI_browser". default, field 'phenotype-AMR_associated_publications' used indicate source row input file, missing want override single value samples, may provide source name via parameter. species (optional) Name species use phenotype interpretation. default, field 'phenotype-organism' assumed specify species row input file, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single species name via parameter. ab (optional) Name antibiotic use phenotype interpretation. default, field 'phenotype-antibiotic_name' assumed specify antibiotic row input file, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single antibiotic name via parameter. interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class 'sir'. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class 'sir'. interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class 'sir' coded 'R' (nonwildtype) 'S' (wildtype).

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_ast
text: data frame processed AST data, including additional columns: id: biological sample identifier (renamed phenotype-BioSample_ID specified column). spp_pheno: species phenotype, formatted using .mo function. drug_agent: antibiotic used test, formatted using .ab function. mic: minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) value, formatted using .mic function. disk: disk diffusion measurement (mm), formatted using .disk function. method: AST method (e.g., "broth dilution", "disk diffusion", "Etest", "agar dilution"). Expected values based NCBI/EBI antibiogram specification. platform: AST platform/instrument (e.g., "Vitek", "Phoenix", "Sensititre"). guideline: AST standard recorded input file used AST assay. pheno_eucast: phenotype newly interpreted EUCAST human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_clsi: phenotype newly interpreted CLSI human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. ecoff: phenotype newly interpreted ECOFF (S/R), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_provided: original phenotype interpretation provided input file. source: source data point (renamed publications field input file, replaced single value passed 'source' parameter).

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the EBI AMR web portal — import_ebi_ast
text: function process data, optionally interpret results based MIC disk diffusion data. assumes input file tab-delimited text file (e.g., TSV) CSV (may compressed) parses relevant columns (antibiotic names, species names, MIC disk data) suitable classes using AMR package. optionally can use AMR package interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) based EUCAST CLSI guidelines (human breakpoints /ECOFF).

what: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
text: function imports processes genotyping results Kleborate (https://github.com/klebgenomics/Kleborate), extracting antimicrobial resistance determinants mapping standardised drug classes.

title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
code: import_kleborate(   input_table,   sample_col = "strain",   kleborate_class_table = kleborate_classes,   hgvs = TRUE )

title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
text: input_table character string specifying dataframe path Kleborate results table (TSV format). sample_col character string specifying column identifies samples dataset (default strain). kleborate_class_table tibble containing reference table mapping Kleborate drug class column names (Kleborate_Class) standardised drug classes (drug_class). Defaults kleborate_classes, provided internally. hgvs Logical indicating whether expect mutations HGVS format (used Kleborate releases since v3.1.3). Default TRUE, expects mutations formatted e.g. "GyrA:p.S83F". Set FALSE results generated using older versions mutations formatted e.g. "GyrA_83F".

title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
text: tibble containing processed AMR determinants drug classes AMRgen compatible.

title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate
text: function performs following steps: Reads Kleborate output table. Transforms Kleborate output long form (.e., one AMR determinant per row). Maps Kleborate drug classes standardised drug class names. processing ensures compatibility downstream AMRgen analysis workflows.

title: Import and Process Kleborate Results — import_kleborate

what: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the NCBI AST browser — import_ncbi_ast
title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the NCBI AST browser — import_ncbi_ast
text: function imports antibiotic susceptibility testing (AST) dataset, processes data, optionally interprets results based MIC disk diffusion data. assumes input file tab-delimited text file (e.g., TSV) CSV (may compressed) parses relevant columns (antibiotic names, species names, MIC disk data) suitable classes using AMR package. optionally can use AMR package interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) based EUCAST CLSI guidelines (human breakpoints /ECOFF). expected columns found warnings given, interpretation may possible.

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the NCBI AST browser — import_ncbi_ast
code: import_ncbi_ast(   input,   sample_col = "BioSample",   source = NULL,   species = NULL,   ab = NULL,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the NCBI AST browser — import_ncbi_ast
text: input string representing dataframe, path input file, containing AST data NCBI antibiogram format. files can downloaded NCBI AST browser, e.g. https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pathogens/ast#Pseudomonas%20aeruginosa sample_col string indicating name column sample identifiers. NULL, assume 'BioSample'. source (optional) single value record source data points, e.g. "NCBI_browser". default, field 'BioProject' used indicate source row input file, missing want override single value samples, may provide source name via parameter. species (optional) Name species use phenotype interpretation. default, field 'Scientific name' assumed specify species row input file, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single species name via parameter. ab (optional) Name antibiotic use phenotype interpretation. default, field 'Antibiotic' assumed specify antibiotic row input file, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single antibiotic name via parameter. interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class 'sir'. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class 'sir'. interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class 'sir' coded 'R' (nonwildtype) 'S' (wildtype).

title: Import and process antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data from the NCBI AST browser — import_ncbi_ast
text: data frame processed AST data, including additional columns: id: biological sample identifier (renamed BioSample specified column). spp_pheno: species phenotype, formatted using .mo function. drug_agent: antibiotic used test, formatted using .ab function. mic: minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) value, formatted using .mic function. disk: disk diffusion measurement (mm), formatted using .disk function. method: AST method (e.g., "broth dilution", "disk diffusion", "Etest", "agar dilution"). Expected values based NCBI/EBI antibiogram specification. Note NCBI allows "MIC" synonym "broth dilution" function convert "MIC" "broth dilution" import. platform: AST platform/instrument (e.g., "Vitek", "Phoenix", "Sensititre"). guideline: AST standard recorded input file used AST assay. pheno_eucast: phenotype newly interpreted EUCAST human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_clsi: phenotype newly interpreted CLSI human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. ecoff: phenotype newly interpreted ECOFF (S/R), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_provided: original phenotype interpretation provided input file. source: source data point (renamed BioProject input file, replaced single value passed 'source' parameter).

code: import_phoenix_ast(   input,   sample_col = NULL,   drug_col = NULL,   mic_col = NULL,   sir_col = NULL,   species_col = NULL,   species = NULL,   ab = NULL,   use_expertized = TRUE,   source = NULL,   instrument_guideline = NULL,   interpret_eucast = FALSE,   interpret_clsi = FALSE,   interpret_ecoff = FALSE )

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from BD Phoenix instruments — import_phoenix_ast
text: input Path Phoenix export file (XLS, XLSX, TXT, TSV), data frame. sample_col Name 1-based column index sample ID column. NULL (default), auto-detected common names ("Sample", "ID", "Isolate"). single-isolate files sample column, filename used sample ID. drug_col Name 1-based column index drug/antimicrobial column. NULL (default), auto-detected names "Antimicrobial", "Drug", etc. mic_col Name 1-based column index MIC column. NULL (default), auto-detected names "MIC", "MIC Concentration", etc. sir_col Name 1-based column index S//R interpretation column. NULL (default), auto-detected based use_expertized: prefers "Final (SIR)", "Expert (SIR)" TRUE; prefers "Interp" FALSE. species_col Name 1-based column index species/organism column. NULL (default), auto-detected names "Organism", "Species", etc. Ignored species supplied. species Optional fixed species string applied rows (parsed via AMR::.mo()). Overrides species_col. Required files without organism column. ab Optional antibiotic filter/override passed interpret_ast(). use_expertized TRUE (default), prefer expert/final interpretation column raw instrument interpretation present sir_col specified. source Optional source label recorded source output column. instrument_guideline Optional guideline string recorded guideline column (e.g. "CLSI 2018", "EUCAST 2024"). interpret_eucast Interpret MIC values EUCAST human breakpoints (default FALSE). interpret_clsi Interpret MIC values CLSI human breakpoints (default FALSE). interpret_ecoff Interpret MIC values ECOFF values (default FALSE).

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from BD Phoenix instruments — import_phoenix_ast
text: Standardised long-format AST data frame

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from BD Phoenix instruments — import_phoenix_ast
text: Phoenix instruments can export data different file formats. function handles named-header exports (e.g. tab-delimited CLSI reports columns "Antimicrobial", "MIC Concentration", "Final (SIR)") positional/headerless exports (XLS files column headers). Column detection automatic based common Phoenix header name patterns, column can overridden supplying name 1-based index via drug_col, mic_col, sir_col, sample_col, species_col. headerless files (detected standard column names recognised), function assumes standard Phoenix positional layout: column 1 = sample ID, column 2 = species, column 3 = drug, column 4 = MIC, column 5 = instrument interpretation, column 6 = expert/final interpretation. Drug names standardised using AMR::.ab(), handles abbreviated, full, language-specific names (e.g. "Fosfomycin mit G6P"). Species names standardised using AMR::.mo(). tab-delimited files, trailing non-data sections commonly appended Phoenix (e.g. "Resistance Markers", "Expert Triggered Rules") automatically discarded.

title: Import and process antimicrobial phenotype data exported from BD Phoenix instruments — import_phoenix_ast
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Named-header export (e.g. CLSI report, auto-detected) pheno <- import_phoenix_ast("phoenix_clsi_report.txt",   species = "Escherichia coli", instrument_guideline = "CLSI 2018" )  # Positional/headerless XLS export (auto-detected) pheno <- import_phoenix_ast("phoenix_export.xls")  # Override column detection when names differ from defaults pheno <- import_phoenix_ast("phoenix_export.xlsx",   drug_col = "Antibiotic", mic_col = "Result",   sir_col = "SIR_final", sample_col = "IsolateID",   species_col = "Organism" ) } # }

what: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_ast
title: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_ast
text: function applies human EUCAST CLSI breakpoints, /ECOFF, interpret antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) data.

title: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_ast
code: interpret_ast(   ast,   interpret_ecoff = TRUE,   interpret_eucast = TRUE,   interpret_clsi = TRUE,   species = NULL,   ab = NULL )

title: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_ast
text: ast tibble containing AST measures standard AMRgen format, output import_ast. must contain assay measurements columns 'mic' (class mic) /'disk'. Interpretation requires organism (column 'spp_pheno' class 'mo', single value passed via 'species' parameter) antibiotic (column 'drug_agent' class 'ab', single value passed via 'ab' parameter). interpret_ecoff logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret wildtype vs nonwildtype status row based MIC disk diffusion values, epidemiological cut-(ECOFF) values. reported new column ecoff, class 'sir' coded 'R' (nonwildtype) 'S' (wildtype). interpret_eucast logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, EUCAST human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_eucast, class 'sir'. interpret_clsi logical value (default FALSE). TRUE, function interpret susceptibility phenotype (SIR) row based MIC disk diffusion values, CLSI human breakpoints. reported new column pheno_clsi, class 'sir'. species (optional) Name species use phenotype interpretation. default, spp_pheno field input file assumed specify species sample, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single species name via parameter. ab (optional) Name antibiotic use phenotype interpretation. default, drug_agent field input file assumed specify antibiotic sample, missing want override interpretation step, may provide single antibiotic name via parameter.

title: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_ast
text: copy input table, additional columns: pheno_eucast: phenotype newly interpreted EUCAST human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. pheno_clsi: phenotype newly interpreted CLSI human breakpoint standards (S//R), based MIC disk diffusion data. ecoff: phenotype newly interpreted ECOFF (S/R), based MIC disk diffusion data. spp_pheno: species phenotype, formatted using .mo function (either taken input table, single value specified 'species' parameter). drug_agent: antibiotic used test, formatted using .ab function (either taken input table, single value specified 'ab' parameter)..

title: Interpret antimicrobial susceptibility phenotype data in a standard format tibble — interpret_ast
code: # import without re-interpreting resistance pheno <- import_ncbi_ast(ecoli_ast_raw) #> Warning: Expected AST method column 'Laboratory typing method' not found in input #> Warning: Expected column 'BioProject' not found in input head(pheno) #> # A tibble: 6 × 29 #>   id           drug_agent    mic  disk guideline method  platform pheno_provided #>   <chr>        <ab>        <mic> <dsk> <chr>     <chr>   <chr>    <sir>          #> 1 SAMN11638310 CIP        256.00    NA CLSI      broth … NA         R            #> 2 SAMN05729964 CIP         64.00    NA CLSI      Etest   Etest      R            #> 3 SAMN10620111 CIP        >=4.00    NA CLSI      broth … NA         R            #> 4 SAMN10620168 CIP        >=4.00    NA CLSI      broth … NA         R            #> 5 SAMN10620104 CIP        <=0.25    NA CLSI      broth … NA         S            #> 6 SAMN10620102 CIP        >=4.00    NA CLSI      broth … NA         R            #> # ℹ 21 more variables: spp_pheno <mo>, `Organism group` <chr>, #> #   `Scientific name` <chr>, `Isolation type` <chr>, Location <chr>, #> #   `Isolation source` <chr>, Isolate <chr>, Antibiotic <chr>, #> #   `Resistance phenotype` <chr>, `Measurement sign` <chr>, `MIC (mg/L)` <dbl>, #> #   `Disk diffusion (mm)` <lgl>, `Laboratory typing platform` <chr>, #> #   Vendor <chr>, `Laboratory typing method version or reagent` <chr>, #> #   `Testing standard` <chr>, `Create date` <dttm>, pheno_clsi_mic <sir>, …  # interpret phenotypes pheno <- interpret_ast(pheno)  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pheno <- read_csv("AST.csv") %>%   # convert antibiotic field to 'drug_agent' of class 'ab'   mutate(drug_agent = as.ab(antibiotic)) %>%   mutate(mic = paste0(sign, MIC)) %>%   mutate(mic = as.mic(mic)) # create a single 'mic' column of class 'mic'  pheno <- interpret_ast(pheno, species = "Escherichia coli") } # }

what: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
title: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
text: function creates ggplot object visualizing logistic regression coefficients 95% confidence intervals. Significant markers highlighted based specified p-value threshold.

title: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
code: plot_estimates(   tbl,   sig = 0.05,   sig_colors = c(`FALSE` = "grey", `TRUE` = "blue4"),   x_title = "Coefficient (95% CI)",   y_title = "Variant",   title = NULL,   axis_label_size = 9,   marker_order = NULL )

title: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
text: tbl data frame tibble containing logistic regression results. Expected columns : marker: name marker (e.g., variable name). pval: p-value marker. ci.lower: lower bound confidence interval. ci.upper: upper bound confidence interval. est: estimated coefficient. sig (optional) significance threshold p-values. Defaults 0.05. sig_colors (optional) vector two colors represent significant non-significant estimates. x_title (optional) title x-axis. Defaults "Coefficient (95% CI)". y_title (optional) title y-axis. Defaults "Variant". title (optional) main title plot. NULL, title added. axis_label_size (optional) font size axis labels. Defaults 9. marker_order (optional) Vector indicating order markers plotted y-axis.

title: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
text: ggplot object showing logistic regression coefficients confidence intervals. Significant markers (p-value < sig) colored differently.

title: Plot Estimates from a Table of Results — plot_estimates
code: tbl <- tibble::tibble(   marker = c("(Intercept)", "var1", "var2", "var3"),   pval = c(0.1, 0.03, 0.2, 0.04),   ci.lower = c(-0.2, 0.1, -0.3, 0.2),   ci.upper = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.4, 1.1),   est = c(0.2, 0.5, 0.1, 0.7) )  plot_estimates(tbl)

title: Generate PPV Plot — ppv
text: function generates plot showing summaries phenotype results (assay distributions, phenotype category percentages, /predictive value phenotype) combination markers observed data.

title: Generate PPV Plot — ppv
code: ppv(   binary_matrix = NULL,   min_set_size = 2,   order = "ppv",   geno_table,   pheno_table,   antibiotic = NULL,   drug_class_list = NULL,   geno_sample_col = NULL,   pheno_sample_col = NULL,   sir_col = NULL,   ecoff_col = "ecoff",   marker_col = "marker",   colours_ppv = c(R = "maroon", NWT = "navy"),   SIR_col = c(S = "#3CAEA3", I = "#F6D55C", R = "#ED553B"),   upset_grid = FALSE,   marker_label_space = NULL,   plot_category = TRUE,   print_category_counts = TRUE,   plot_ppv = TRUE,   plot_assay = FALSE,   assay = NULL,   boxplot_col = "grey",   species = NULL,   bp_site = NULL,   guideline = "EUCAST 2025",   bp_S = NULL,   bp_R = NULL,   ecoff_bp = NULL,   pd = position_dodge(width = 0.8) )

title: Generate PPV Plot — ppv
text: binary_matrix data frame containing original binary matrix output get_binary_matrix() function. provided (set NULL), user must specify geno_table, pheno_table, antibiotic, drug_class_list optionally geno_sample_col, pheno_sample_col, sir_col, ecoff_col, marker_col pass get_binary_matrix(). min_set_size integer specifying minimum size gene set included analysis plots. Default 2. marker combinations least number occurrences included plots. order character string indicating order combinations x-axis. Options : "": decreasing frequency combinations "genes": order number genes combination "value": order median assay value (MIC disk zone) combination. "ppv" (default): order PPV estimated combination geno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing genotype data, formatted import_amrfp(). used binary_matrix provided. pheno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing phenotype data, formatted import_ast(). used binary_matrix provided. antibiotic (Required binary_matrix provided) character string specifying antibiotic interest filter phenotype data (optionally retrieve breakpoints). value must match one entries drug_agent column pheno_table. used binary_matrix provided breakpoints required. drug_class_list (relevant binary_matrix provided) provided, AMR pkg used check class name/s associated antibiotic uses (printed screen user can see filtered). geno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name geno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. pheno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name pheno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. sir_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretation (SIR) data. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). provided, first column prefixed "phenotype*" used present, otherwise error thrown.  used binary_matrix provided. ecoff_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretations (SIR) made ECOFF rather clinical breakpoint. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). Default ecoff. Set NULL available.  used binary_matrix provided. marker_col character string specifying column name geno_table containing marker identifiers. Default "marker". used binary_matrix provided. colours_ppv named vector colours plot PPV estimates. names "R", "" "NWT", values valid color names hexadecimal color codes. SIR_col named vector colours percentage bar plot. names phenotype categories (e.g., "R", "", "S"), values valid color names hexadecimal color codes. Default values used AMR package AMR::scale_colour_sir(). upset_grid Logical indicating whether show marker combinations upset plot-style grid (default FALSE, row instead labelled printed list markers). marker_label_space Relative width plotting area provide marker list/grid. (Default NULL, results default value 3 upset_grid=FALSE 1 otherwise). plot_category Logical indicating whether include stacked bar plot showing, marker combination, proportion samples phenotype classification (specified pheno column input file). Default TRUE. print_category_counts Logical indicating whether, plot_category=TRUE, print number strains resistance category marker combination plot. Default FALSE. plot_ppv Logical indicating whether plot estimates positive predictive value, marker combination (default TRUE). plot_assay Logical indicating whether plot distribution MIC/disk assay values, marker combination (default FALSE). Note must also indicate assay column plot ("mic" "disk") via assay. assay character string indicating whether plot MIC disk diffusion data. Must one : NULL: (default) assay data plotted "mic": plot MIC data stored column mic "disk": plot disk diffusion data stored column disk boxplot_col Colour lines box plots summarising MIC distribution marker combination. Default "grey". species Optional. Species name used retrieve clinical breakpoints annotation assay distribution plot. bp_site Optional. Breakpoint site (e.g. "Non-meningitis") used retrieving clinical breakpoints. guideline Guideline used breakpoint lookup. Default "EUCAST 2025". bp_S (optional) S breakpoint add assay distribution plot (numerical). bp_R (optional) R breakpoint add assay distribution plot (numerical). ecoff_bp (optional) ECOFF breakpoint add assay distribution plot (numerical). pd ggplot2::position_dodge() object controlling horizontal spacing points confidence intervals PPV plot. Default position_dodge(width = 0.8).

title: Generate PPV Plot — ppv
text: list containing following elements: plot: grid requested plots binary_matrix: copy genotype-phenotype binary matrix (either provided input generated function) summary: data frame summarizing marker combination observed, including number resistant isolates, positive predictive values, median assay values (interquartile range) relevant.

title: Generate PPV Plot — ppv
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ecoli_geno <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name")  # Generate binary matrix binary_matrix <- get_binary_matrix(   geno_table = ecoli_geno,   pheno_table = ecoli_ast,   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   drug_class_list = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "pheno_clsi",   keep_assay_values = TRUE,   keep_assay_values_from = "mic" )  # Run ppv analysis using this binary_matrix ppv <- ppv(binary_matrix)  # Alternatively, generate binary matrix and run ppv() in one step ppv <- ppv(   geno_table = ecoli_geno,   pheno_table = ecoli_ast,   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   sir_col = "pheno_clsi" ) } # }

text: geno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing genotype data, formatted import_amrfp(). used binary_matrix provided. pheno_table (Required binary_matrix provided) data frame containing phenotype data, formatted import_ast(). used binary_matrix provided. antibiotic (Required binary_matrix provided) character string specifying antibiotic interest filter phenotype data. value must match one entries drug_agent column pheno_table. used binary_matrix provided breakpoints required. drug_class_list (relevant binary_matrix provided) provided, AMR pkg used check class name/s associated antibiotic uses (printed screen user can see filtered). geno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name geno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. pheno_sample_col character string (optional) specifying column name pheno_table containing sample identifiers. Defaults NULL, case assumed first column contains identifiers. used binary_matrix provided. sir_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains resistance interpretation (SIR) data. values "S", "", "R" otherwise interpretable AMR::.sir(). provided, first column prefixed "phenotype*" used present, otherwise error thrown.  used binary_matrix provided. ecoff_col character string specifying column name pheno_table contains ECOFF interpretation phenotype. values interpretable "WT" (wildtype) "NWT" (nonwildtype), "S" / "" / "R". Default ecoff. Set NULL available.  used binary_matrix provided. icat logical indicating whether calculate PPV "" (category exists phenotype column) (default FALSE). marker_col character string specifying column name geno_table containing marker identifiers. Default "marker". used binary_matrix provided. reverse_order logical indicating whether reverse order rows plot, markers ordered lowest R PPV highest (default FALSE, .e. markers ordered highest lowest PPV). binary_matrix data frame containing original binary matrix output get_binary_matrix() function. provided (set NULL), user must specify geno_table, pheno_table, antibiotic, drug_class_list optionally geno_sample_col, pheno_sample_col, sir_col, ecoff_col, marker_col pass get_binary_matrix(). min Minimum number genomes solo marker, include marker plot (default 1). axis_label_size Font size axis labels PPV plot (default 9). pd ggplot2::position_dodge() object controlling horizontal spacing points confidence intervals PPV plot. Default position_dodge(width = 0.8). excludeRanges Vector phenotype categories (comprised "R", "", "NWT") ignore MIC values expressed ranges calculating PPVs. include MICs expressed ranges set NULL. colours_SIR named vector colours percentage bar plot. names phenotype categories (e.g., "R", "", "S"), values valid colour names hexadecimal colour codes. Default values used AMR package AMR::scale_fill_sir(). colours_ppv named vector colours plot PPV estimates. names "R", "", "NWT", values valid colour names hexadecimal colour codes.

title: Perform Solo PPV Analysis for AMR Markers — solo_ppv_analysis
text: list containing following elements: solo_stats: data frame summarizing PPV resistance (R vs S/) NWT (R/vs S), including number positive hits, sample size, PPV, 95% confidence intervals marker. combined_plot: combined ggplot object showing PPV plot solo markers, bar plot phenotype distribution. solo_binary: dataframe binary values indicating presence absence solo markers. solo_binary_norange: dataframe binary values indicating presence absence solo markers, excluding samples MIC disk measures expressed ranges. amr_binary: copy genotype-phenotype binary matrix markers (either provided input generated function) plot_order: Ordered list rows output plot (facilitate alignment plots)

title: Perform Solo PPV Analysis for AMR Markers — solo_ppv_analysis
text: function analyzes predictive power individual AMR markers found 'solo' genome markers associated class. phenotype data matched genotype presence/absence stratified compute PPV resistance non-wild-type interpretations. function also generates plots aid interpretation.

title: Perform Solo PPV Analysis for AMR Markers — solo_ppv_analysis
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ geno_table <- import_amrfp(ecoli_geno_raw, "Name") head(geno_table)  # Generate binary matrix binary_matrix <- get_binary_matrix(   geno_table = geno_table,   pheno_table = ecoli_ast,   antibiotic = "Ciprofloxacin",   drug_class_list = c("Quinolones"),   sir_col = "pheno_clsi",   keep_assay_values = TRUE,   keep_assay_values_from = "mic" )  # Run solo PPV analysis plot analysis using this binary_matrix # (note antibiotic and drug_class list are optional here, and only used # for titling the plot) soloPPV_cipro <- solo_ppv_analysis(binary_matrix = binary_matrix)  soloPPV_cipro$solo_stats soloPPV_cipro$combined_plot } # }

title: Summarise a Phenotype Table — summarise_pheno
text: pheno_table tibble data frame containing phenotype data, format output import_ast. sample_col Character. Name column containing sample identifiers. Default "id". drug_col Character. Name column containing drug agent identifiers. Default "drug_agent". class 'ab' entries annotated full antibiotic names, converted using AMR::.ab. desired behaviour class 'ab', set force_ab=TRUE. mic_col Character. Name column containing MIC data. Default "mic". disk_col Character. Name column containing drug class identifiers. Default "disk". spp_col Character. Name column containing species names Default "spp_pheno". pheno_cols Vector. Vector giving names columns containing categorical phenotype calls (S//R NWT/WT). Default columns beginning "pheno" "ecoff". method_cols Vector. Vector giving names columns containing method source information summarise MIC/disk data. Default c("method", "platform", "guideline", "source"). force_ab Logical. TRUE, attempts convert entries drug_col antibiotic names using AMR::.ab even column class "ab" Default FALSE.
